t_ch: 看来还是猴子更加适合你现在的精神状态！
t_en: Seems like a monkey suits your current mental state better!

t_ch: 趁你的身体还没有彻底反应过来，{RUBY_B#海洋之泰坦}法吉娜{RUBY_E#}的神威已经褪去，你又重新变回了人类的模样。
t_en: Before your body fully reacts, the power of {RUBY_B#Ocean Titan}Phagousa{RUBY_E#} fades away, and you turn back into a human.

t_ch: …说不定，你就是从这样的东西进化而来的呢？
t_en: ...Maybe this is what you evolved from?

t_ch: 离开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 「扎格列斯之手」会挡住你的去路，是否让它回到原来的位置？
t_en: Hand of Zagreus will be blocking your path. Return it to its original position?

t_ch: 重置位置。
t_en: Reset Position

t_ch: 离开。
t_en: Leave

t_ch: 今天的橄榄也不错，你可以尝尝…说起来，赫卡特的探险小队昨天又出城了。
t_en: These olives today taste pretty good. You should try some... By the way, Hecate's expedition squad set off on another adventure yesterday.

t_ch: 嗯？最近他们活动得还挺频繁…哦哦，确实不错，给我称一些吧。
t_en: Yeah, they've been moving around a lot lately... Oh, these olives are really something. I'll have a few more.

t_ch: 听说他们正准备深入探索东北边那片区域，那里很久都没人去过了吧，会不会有什么危险……
t_en: I heard they're prepping for an in-depth exploration up northeast, but that area's been deserted for ages, right? Wonder if there are any dangers lurking...

t_ch: 危险肯定是有的，但那小姑娘的能力你又不是不知道，就算真遇上什么发疯的泰坦眷属，想全身而退也不难吧。
t_en: There are always dangers, but you know how skilled she is, right? Even if she bumps into some wild Titankins, she can probably finagle her way out unscathed.

t_ch: 话虽如此…上个月不是才有个探险队遭遇了黑潮，死伤惨重吗。哎，要不然你给占卜一下？
t_en: True, but... didn't one of the expeditions get struck by the black tide and suffer heavy losses last month? Hmm, maybe you could do a little divination for me?

t_ch: 你啊…行吧，这次给你算便宜点。
t_en: Come on... Fine, I'll cut you a deal this time.

t_ch: 名为卡森德拉的老妇人从怀中掏出一架小天秤放在桌上，又在水果堆中随手拿了苹果、石榴各一颗，分别放在两侧的托盘上。
t_en: The woman named Cassondra pulls out a small scale from her pocket, placing it on the table. Then, she casually picks up an apple and a pomegranate from the fruit pile, setting them on the opposing scale trays.

t_ch: 天秤左右摇晃半晌，最终石榴所在的那侧稍稍翘起。
t_en: The scale teeters for a moment before tipping slightly in favor of the pomegranate.

t_ch: 瞧吧，就说不用担心，塔兰顿说了：「危机如鹰隼时时盘旋于顶，机警如狡狐引其脱出绝境」——只要多留心，总归不会有什么大问题。
t_en: See? Told you not to worry. Remember what Talanton teaches us, "Though danger may swoop like an eagle, let your vigilance guide you like a clever fox." Stay alert, and you'll be just fine.

t_ch: 行了，那我就先走……
t_en: Well, I should get going...

t_ch: 哎，你等等！
t_en: Hey, hold on a sec!

t_ch: 干嘛？就一颗苹果一颗石榴而已，你不会还打算要回去吧？「公正之秤」在上，你可不能克扣我的正当酬劳。
t_en: What's up? It's just an apple and a pomegranate. You're not going to take them back now, are you? Praise the Scale of Justice, this is my fair payment for the service.

t_ch: 哦，塔兰顿肯定不允许我再额外给你几个蜜果尝尝。哎，那可是今天{RUBY_B#黎明}门扉时{RUBY_E#}新采回来的，鲜嫩得很……
t_en: Ah, got it. In that case, I suppose Talanton wouldn't approve of me slipping you a few extra figs. What a pity. They were picked at the {RUBY_B#dawn}Entry Hour{RUBY_E#}, and they look so fresh...

t_ch: 别别，谁说我不要的！我这就帮你品鉴品鉴…嗯，味道确实不错…哎？有贵客上门了呀，我就先不打搅了。
t_en: Oh, wait, wait! I'll never say no to figs! Let me try some for you... Hmm, these do taste great... Oh? Do you have a customer now? I'll leave you to it.

t_ch: 欢迎光临…哎呀，你就是白厄阁下带回来的外邦客人吧？看起来可真讨人喜欢。
t_en: Welcome... Hey, you must be the outlander brought back by Lord Phainon, right? You seem like quite the charming one.

t_ch: 欢迎来到奥赫玛，年轻人，对这边的生活还适应吗？
t_en: Welcome to Okhema, young one. How are you finding life here so far?

t_ch: 感觉还行吧。
t_en: It's okay.

t_ch: 哈哈，初来乍到时大家多少都会有些不适应。不过要是待得久了，你就会发现奥赫玛还是挺宜居的——无论气候、环境还是生活节奏。
t_en: Aha, it can take some time for everyone to get used to things at first, but once you do, you'll see that Okhema is quite cozy — that goes for the weather, the surroundings, and the lifestyle.

t_ch: 很享受这里的生活！
t_en: Living here is a real bliss!

t_ch: 哈哈，那真是太好了。作为受到刻法勒庇佑的圣城，奥赫玛并没有受到黑潮的影响，无论气候、环境还是生活节奏都非常宜人。
t_en: Aha! That's great to hear! This holy city, blessed by Kephale, remains unharmed by the black tide. Over time, you'll find that Okhema is quite cozy — that goes for the weather, the surroundings, and the lifestyle.

t_ch: 有些人不是很友好。
t_en: Some people here seem a bit unfriendly.

t_ch: 这样啊…不过初来乍到时，大家其实多少都会有些不适应的。不过要是待得久了，你就会发现这里的居民其实还都挺好相处的。
t_en: I see... It can take some time for everyone to get used to things at first, but once you do, you'll find that the people here are actually pretty friendly.

t_ch: 希望你和你的同伴能够尽快融入这里，年轻人。以及如果有什么需要的话，我非常乐意提供一些力所能及的帮助。
t_en: I hope you and your companions settle in quickly, young one. If you need anything, don't hesitate to ask for my help.

t_ch: 又见面了，年轻人。要来看看今天新采的水果吗？
t_en: Here we meet again, young one. Interested in checking out the fresh fruits picked today?

t_ch: 我有什么能帮你做的吗？
t_en: Do you need my help with anything?

t_ch: 哈哈，谢谢你，年轻人。但用不着这么客气，我本来就很喜欢小孩子，只要你能多来和我聊聊天，我就已经很开心啦。
t_en: Haha, thank you, but there's no need to be a stranger, young one. I enjoy spending time with you young folks. Feel free to drop by anytime for a chat, and that would already make me very happy.

t_ch: 不过…这么说来我倒是有个不情之请。半年前我的女儿和女婿一起跟随一支探险队出城探索，至今了无音讯。
t_en: But then again... I do have a small favor to ask. Half a year ago, my daughter and son-in-law went on an expedition away from the city, and they haven't been heard from since.

t_ch: 虽然…但如果你之后有机会出城的话，可以顺带帮我留意一下他们的消息吗？真的非常感谢！
t_en: So... if you ever find yourself leaving the city, could you be on the lookout for any information about them? I'd really appreciate it!

t_ch: 我还有事，先走了。
t_en: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

t_ch: 好呀，欢迎你随时再来，年轻人。
t_en: Sure! You're always welcome here, young one.

t_ch: 想看看店里的水果。
t_en: I'd like to check out the fresh fruits here.

t_ch: 当然没问题，如果有什么想吃的，你可以直接尝尝。
t_en: Sure thing. Feel free to sample anything that catches your eye.

t_ch: 苹果。
t_en: Apple, please.

t_ch: 苹果是奥赫玛——或者说整个翁法罗斯——最常见的水果，而你手中这个是经过多次改良的品种，营养更加丰富，保鲜时间也更长。
t_en: Apples are the most common fruit here in Okhema — perhaps in all of Amphoreus. This apple in your hand is from a cultivar that has been genetically improved multiple times, bred to be extra nutritious and stay fresh for longer.

t_ch: 无论是鲜果，还是其经过加工后制成的果酱、果脯、果干或罐头，都是深受探险者们喜爱的补给。
t_en: Whether it's fresh apples, apple jams, preserves, or dried apples, they're all quite popular as supplies among explorers.

t_ch: 你看看手中色泽鲜艳、光可鉴人的苹果，一口咬了下去——
t_en: Gazing at the brightly-colored and resplendent apple in your hand, you take a hearty bite...

t_ch: 「咔嚓——」如此清脆可口！「咔嚓——」这般甘甜多汁！
t_en: *crunch* So crisp and juicy! *crunch* So sweet and refreshing!

t_ch: 「咔嚓咔嚓咔嚓——」你三口并作两口，眨眼间便将拳头大小的苹果啃了个干净。
t_en: *crunch* *crunch* You keep taking bites out of the apple, and the fist-sized fruit is consumed almost instantly.
...*burp*

t_ch: 哈哈，看来还算和你的口味。承蒙惠顾，<unbreak>20</unbreak>利衡币。
t_en: Aha, looks like you like it. That'll be <unbreak>20</unbreak> Balance Coins. Thank you for your patronage!

t_ch: 石榴。
t_en: Pomegranate, please.

t_ch: 石榴啊…这种水果可是悬锋人的最爱。据说在他们的传统中，石榴汁因为色泽好似鲜血，就被视作可以补气回血、活络身心的灵药呢。
t_en: Pomegranate... that's the favorite fruit for Kremnoans. Pomegranate juice, with its blood-like color, is seen as a revitalizing elixir for both body and mind in their customs.

t_ch: 说来黄金裔中那位万敌阁下就来自悬锋城，他偶尔路过这里时，总会买些石榴。托他的福，这种水果的销量一直不错。
t_en: Speaking of which, Lord Mydeimos among the Chrysos Heirs hails from Castrum Kremnos. He always picks up some pomegranates whenever he swings by. Thanks to him, the sales of pomegranates have been thriving.

t_ch: 你剥开紧实光滑的果皮，只见颗颗红宝石般的饱满籽粒堆叠镶嵌，好似某种别致的工艺品。
t_en: You peel back the smooth and tight skin, revealing the ruby-red seeds neatly arranged like an intricate artifact.

t_ch: 你抓起一把石榴籽塞了满口——真的假的？！果核竟然也能吃，而且口感还和仙人快乐茶里的珍珠如此相似！
t_en: You scoop up a handful of those seeds and pop them in your mouth... Really!? Even the cores are edible, boasting a texture akin to the sticky rice balls found in Immortal's Delight.

t_ch: 诶呀，看起来你应该会和那位万敌阁下非常有共同语言呢。承蒙惠顾，<unbreak>100</unbreak>利衡币。
t_en: Oh, it looks like you and Lord Mydeimos share a similar taste. That'll be <unbreak>100</unbreak> Balance Coins. Thank you for your patronage!

t_ch: 蜜果。
t_en: Figs, please.

t_ch: 这是麦迪特瑞恩蜜果。传说中这种果子为法吉娜所钟爱，用它制成的秘酿能给人带来精神与肉体的双重疗愈。
t_en: This is the Mediterrana fig. Legend has it that it was Phagousa's favorite, and the ambrosia made from it could heal both body and soul.

t_ch: 这种蜜果原本已经随着城邦的覆灭而绝迹多年，但三年前有支探险队成功深入麦迪特瑞恩城的废墟，带回了尚未腐烂的枯种。
t_en: This fig had been extinct for years after that city's fall, but three years ago, an expedition braved the ruins of Mediterrana and retrieved withered seeds that hadn't rotted.

t_ch: 后来「智慧花园」的园丁们提取了其中的生命质素，对那些种子进行改良与再培育，才又有了现在的麦迪特瑞恩蜜果。
t_en: Later, the Gardeners in the Garden of Wisdom extracted the life essence within, improved and re-cultivated those seeds, and recreated the Mediterrana figs you see today.

t_ch: 你看看手中紫红色的梨形果实，轻松地撕开薄如蝉翼的果皮，露出其中的鲜红嫩肉。
t_en: Examining the pear-shaped purplish fruit in your hand, you easily peel back the extraordinarily thin skin and expose the vibrant red flesh inside.

t_ch: 只消一小口，唇齿暗自生香；又一口，喉舌余韵回甘；再一口，脏腑如沐清泉。
t_en: A small nip reveals a deep fragrance that envelops your lips and tongue. Another nip leaves a sweet lingering aftertaste. Another nip refreshes your innards like a cool spring.

t_ch: 整个果子下肚，你感觉自己整个人神清气爽，所有疲惫仿佛都消失不见。
t_en: Upon eating the entire fruit, a sense of rejuvenation washes over you, as if all traces of fatigue have dissipated.

t_ch: 怎么样，这种蜜果是不是很神奇？可惜它的产量不是很高，所以价格稍贵一些。那么承蒙惠顾，<unbreak>500</unbreak>利衡币。
t_en: Pretty incredible, right? Unfortunately, it's a bit pricey due to its limited availability. That'll be <unbreak>500</unbreak> Balance Coins. Thank you for your patronage!

t_ch: 没什么想买的了。
t_en: I think I'm good for now.

t_ch: 好呀，欢迎你随时再来，年轻人。
t_en: Sure! You're always welcome here, young one.

t_ch: 异乡人，你好，吃了吗？
t_en: Hello, outlander. Have you eaten yet?

t_ch: 还没呢。
t_en: Not yet.

t_ch: 吉奥里亚告诉我们，饿着肚子打不赢。等我职歇，可以来找我，给你做点。
t_en: Georios says you shouldn't fight while hungry. When I'm done with work, come find me. I'll make you something to eat.

t_ch: 吃了，你呢？
t_en: Yeah, I did. How about you?

t_ch: 还没吃，吉奥里亚告诉我们，饿着肚子打不赢。等我职歇，做点东西吃。
t_en: Not yet. Georios says you shouldn't fight while hungry. When I'm done with work, I'll make myself something to eat.

t_ch: 正好你来了，异乡人。帮我想想。
t_en: Now that you're here, outlander. Help me with this.

t_ch: 门扉时三刻，我打到一只嘟噜。串成串，一定好吃。问你，盐烤？酱烤？或者……
t_en: I caught a Dollo at three quints past the Entry Hour, and it would make great skewers. Should I roast it with salt or with sauce? Or...

t_ch: 达利安！别在那鬼扯了！锋狼都溜进来了！
t_en: Stop talking nonsense over there, Dorian! The direwolves are coming in!

t_ch: 坏了，等我一下。
t_en: Oops! Just one moment.

t_ch: 一头野兽窜进了城里，你本想出手帮忙，但这个卫兵几矛就解决了问题。
t_en: A beast scurries into the city. Just as you are about to intervene, the guard swiftly deals with the intruder in a few strikes.

t_ch: 结束后，他立刻就被叫走了，过了好久才垂头丧气地回来。
t_en: Once the turmoil settles, he is immediately summoned elsewhere and only comes back after a while, looking downcast.

t_ch: 嗯…唉……
t_en: Ugh... Hmm...

t_ch: 被训了？
t_en: Got told off?

t_ch: 让队长训了，卫士工作不能懈怠，不能拿安全开玩笑。唉，只好把嘟噜炖掉，请客道歉。
t_en: Got told off by the captain for slacking off as a guard and not taking safety seriously. So I had to stew the Dollo for everyone to make up for it.

t_ch: 你挺能打啊！
t_en: You fought well!

t_ch: 谢谢，我在工作，这只是基本要求。一码归一码，还是被训了。唉，只好把嘟噜炖掉，请客道歉。
t_en: Thank you. That's the basic requirement of my work. But I won't cancel out the fault that got me in trouble, so I had to stew the Dollo for everyone to make up for it.

t_ch: 异乡人，不好意思。要是你饿了，请你吃别的。
t_en: I'm sorry, outlander. If you're hungry, I'll cook something else for you.

t_ch: 自我介绍，我是，达利安，吉奥里亚的子民。以前是厨子，现在是卫士。
t_en: Let me introduce myself. I'm Dorian, a child of Georios. I used to be a cook, and now I'm a guard.

t_ch: 我会，保卫奥赫玛的安全，不保卫的时候，我会做饭。
t_en: I'll be protecting Okhema, and when I'm not, I'll be cooking.

t_ch: 之后，就算认识了，不会拦你。
t_en: Now that we know each other, I won't stop you in the future anymore.

t_ch: 嗯，今天没有异常。
t_en: Nothing strange going on today.

t_ch: 啊，异乡人，你好，吃了吗？
t_en: Ah, it's you, outlander. Have you eaten yet?

t_ch: 我来找你聊聊天。
t_en: I'm here for a little chat.

t_ch: 好，正好我闲，要长蘑菇了。
t_en: Good, I'm free now. I'm so bored that I might as well be growing mushrooms.

t_ch: 为什么不当厨师了？
t_en: Why did you stop being a cook?

t_ch: 因为，我没得选。
t_en: Because I didn't have a choice.

t_ch: 我以前，也是异乡人。刚来奥赫玛，元老院的移民官，问我以前做什么。我说，是厨师，是高级厨师。
t_en: I was an outlander too. When I came to Okhema, the immigration officer from the Council of Elders asked me what I did, and I told them I was a cook, and a good one.

t_ch: 移民官说：「吉奥里亚给了你强健的体魄，你却只拿它来做饭？」
t_en: Then he scolded me, "Georios blessed you with a strong physique, but all you do is whip up meals?"

t_ch: 他们塞给我，盔甲和武器。让我在这，每天站很久。这工作，一点都不适合我。
t_en: They shoved armor and weapon into my hands and told me to stand here for long hours every day. But this job isn't suited for me at all.

t_ch: 自信点，你挺厉害的。
t_en: Come on! You're pretty skilled.

t_ch: 我当厨师更厉害…但他们说，山之民当厨师，太浪费了。
t_en: I'm even more skilled in the kitchen... but they said it's not right for a Mountain Dweller to be a cook. It's a waste of my talents.

t_ch: 卫兵是个伟大的职业。
t_en: Being a guard is a very important profession.

t_ch: 厨师也很伟大。和卫兵一样，都能让人，身体健康。
t_en: But cooking is important too. Like guards, cooks help keep people alive and healthy.

t_ch: 我懂，山之民当卫兵，更符合，人们的期待。而且，悄悄做饭，我也还行。
t_en: I know, being a guard sounds more like what a Mountain Dweller should do. Plus, I can cook in secret. I can live with that.

t_ch: 吉奥里亚创造我，我不该奢求更多。但我还是难受，想哭。但我是吉奥里亚的子民，哭不出来。
t_en: Georios made me, and I shouldn't ask for more, but it still feels bad. I want to cry. But I'm a child of Georios, and I mustn't cry.

t_ch: 讲讲做饭的故事！
t_en: Tell me more about your cooking stories!

t_ch: 有很多能说的，我想想。嗯……
t_en: There's a lot. Let me think, hmhm...

t_ch: 奥赫玛的菜色，普通，但是诚实。想起以前见过，信仰扎格列斯的人，卖粥。
t_en: Okhema's food is simple but honest. I remember meeting a believer of Zagreus who sold congee.

t_ch: 招牌写着，鱼肉粥。吃起来不像。我问，是鱼肉吗？他说：「不全是」
t_en: Their sign said "fish meat congee," but it didn't taste like fish. So, I asked, "Is it fish?" The cook said, "Well, not a hundred per cent."

t_ch: 混了什么？「混了云羊肉。」混了多少？「一半一半。」一半是多少？「一条鱼，一只云羊。」
t_en: "What else did you add?" "Just some cloudsheep." "How much?" "Half and half, that is." "What do you mean by half?" "One fish, one cloudsheep."

t_ch: 我到过，悬锋城。在那边做饭，很累。
t_en: I've been to Castrum Kremnos. Cooking there is tiring.

t_ch: 他们说，我不能用，肉以外的东西，做饭。为了献给尼卡多利。
t_en: They said I can't cook with anything that's not meat, because it's for Nikador.

t_ch: 后来才知道，没有这条规定。
t_en: Later, I learned there is no such rule.

t_ch: 以前，瑟希斯的信众，会来找我，教我怎么做菜，对身体好。
t_en: Cerces' disciples would come to me in the past. They told me how to cook food that is healthy for the body.

t_ch: 但他们说的，不一样。有人说，水果在餐前吃更好，有人说，之后吃更好。
t_en: But what they said was confusing. Some said to eat fruit before meals, while others said after.

t_ch: 我猜不透，所以在菜里加水果。大家吃一口菜，吃一口水果。
t_en: I can't figure them out, so I started adding fruits to dishes. A bite of the meal, then a bite of fruit.

t_ch: 你一定好奇，我在这里，怎么做饭。
t_en: You might wonder how I cook now in this city.

t_ch: 简单的，赞美吉奥里亚，架锅，赞美吉奥里亚，炖，赞美吉奥里亚，装盘。
t_en: It's simple. Praise Georios, prepare the pot. Praise Georios, cook the stew. Praise Georios, serve the food.

t_ch: 不管哪种食材，炖出来，味道都不会差。赞美吉奥里亚。
t_en: Everything tastes great when stewed. Praise Georios.

t_ch: 我做过的，最好吃的东西，全烧角猪。
t_en: The best dish I ever had was a whole roasted hornswine.

t_ch: 材料只有角猪，和一捆绿菜，老家的人，吃了两天，夸了一整个光历年。
t_en: The ingredients are just a hornswine and a bunch of veggies. My villagers feasted on it for two days and talked about it for a year.

t_ch: 现在，做不出来了。开始手生。没有角猪。
t_en: Can't make it now. No hornswines here, and I'm not as skilled anymore.

t_ch: 我做过的，最失败的菜，刚当上卫兵，炖的粥。
t_en: My worst dish was when I started as a guard. I made stew.

t_ch: 没有炊具，用土烧。没有材料，四周找。难喝到塞纳托斯向我招手，只好倒掉。
t_en: No pots, so I made do with soil. No ingredients, so I had to find some on the spot. The stew was so awful that Thanatos was waving at me, so I had to throw it out.

t_ch: 好在，没有浪费。野生大地兽，被吸引来了，现在住在城里。
t_en: Good thing it wasn't wasted. It attracted wild dromas, and now they live in the city.

t_ch: 讲讲守城的故事？
t_en: How about sharing some stories about guarding the city?

t_ch: 守城无聊，没有什么能说的。
t_en: Guarding city is boring. Not much to tell.

t_ch: …偶尔有东西来。有坏东西，也有人。我做饭的时候，更多。
t_en: ...Trouble comes sometimes. Bad things, bad people. More bad things when I cook.

t_ch: 我只负责，拦下来。之后黄金裔，还有元老院，会处理。就这些了，守城无聊。
t_en: My job is just to stop them, then the Chrysos Heirs and the Council of Elders take over. That's all. Guarding the city is boring.

t_ch: 希望永远没有能说的。
t_en: I hope I never get new stories about guarding the city.

t_ch: 没事啦，你先忙。
t_en: Well, that's it for now. I'll leave you to your duties.

t_ch: 不忙，等你饿了，再来找我，保证好吃。
t_en: No duties. Come to me when you're hungry. I'll cook something yummy.

t_ch: 没事啦，你先忙。
t_en: Well, that's it for now. I'll leave you to your duties.

t_ch: 不忙，等你饿了，再来找我，保证好吃。
t_en: No duties. Come to me when you're hungry. I'll cook something yummy.

t_ch: 饿了，有什么吃的吗？
t_en: I'm starving. Got anything to eat?

t_ch: 有，刚出锅的。拿着。
t_en: Yeah, fresh from the pot. Take it.

t_ch: 没了，还在做，晚点再来吧。
t_en: No, not ready yet. Come back later.

t_ch: 欢迎来到刻法勒广场——看见那群盲信{RUBY_B#全知之神}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}的蠢材了没？对着石头、泥巴和颜料做成的偶像倒头便拜，幻想着自己也像故事中的人一样可以得到神明的垂青。
t_en: Welcome to Kephale Plaza. See those dim-witted believers devoted to {RUBY_B#God of Omniscience}Kephale{RUBY_E#}? They're all about praying to idols made of stone, mud, and paint, imagining that they would receive the gods' blessing like heroes from legends.

t_ch: 教你一招——如果他们非要拉你加入这些仪式，你内心里虽不情愿，但又觉得盛情难却、不知如何是好，那就说自己是{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}的信徒。包你在所有的迷信场合都能进退自如！
t_en: Here's a tip: If they try to rope you into their rituals and you're not feeling it, but you're not sure how to decline their passionate offers, just tell them you follow {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}. It's the perfect excuse to sidestep those awkward moments!

t_ch: …没懂你的逻辑。
t_en: ...I don't quite get it.

t_ch: 假意改信，日后再悔过？
t_en: Pretend to convert, then repent later?

t_ch: 塞纳托斯能止小儿夜啼？
t_en: Can Thanatos terrify crying children into silence?

t_ch: 嗐，没有那么复杂！{RUBY_B#神明}泰坦{RUBY_E#}不过是自然现象当中的一类，塞纳托斯唯一的好处，就是它自动排斥一切繁文缛节。你若是去坟地里办一场演唱会，于{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}而言，也一样无伤大雅。
t_en: Hah, it's not that complicated. The {RUBY_B#gods}Titans{RUBY_E#} are just a type of natural phenomena. One good thing about Thanatos is that they automatically repel all mortal cultural codes. Even if you decide to throw a party in the graveyard, it wouldn't bother {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} one bit.

t_ch: 其他泰坦的信徒可就没有这么宽容喽。人们都说，刻法勒厌恶黑暗——结果刻法勒的圣城就连个真正意义的{RUBY_B#夜幕}晚上{RUBY_E#}都没有！…理解了吗，迷信就是这么过分。
t_en: The disciples of other Titans aren't so easy-going. They all claim Kephale can't stand darkness. And now you see this holy city of Kephale doesn't even have a proper {RUBY_B#night}evening{RUBY_E#}! ...Do you get it now? That's how unreasonable superstitions get?

t_ch: 这好像确实是个问题。
t_en: Sounds like a problem indeed.

t_ch: 你说得未免有点夸张了。
t_en: That's a bit too exaggerated.

t_ch: 轻信你，那也是一种迷信吧？
t_en: Falling for your words... isn't that a superstition in itself?

t_ch: 哈哈，倒也不必较真！俗话说，「矫枉必须过正」——要是没有我们站在风俗的另一端，圣城奥赫玛，恐怕难免要变得过于神圣…那住着多难受啊？
t_en: Haha, no need to get all serious! Like they say, "when you push something, you gotta push it far." Without us standing on the other side of morality's scales, the holy city Okhema would become far too holy... and that'd be a pain to live in, don't you think?

t_ch: 都说历史是人们对往事一致认定的说法…这让我对世界的未来忧心忡忡。不论什么年代，人们总是在重复同样的错误。
t_en: They say history tends to reflect the collective views of the past... and that's what worries me about the future. Regardless of the era, humans just can't seem to break free from the same mistakes.

t_ch: 不过，你也不用太担心；只要黄金裔还打算将泰坦取而代之，只要普通人里还有像我这样的冒牌信徒，奥赫玛的气氛就还不至于难以忍受…如果他们声称自己信的是{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}就更好了！
t_en: But hey, don't stress too much. As long as there are Chrysos Heirs aiming to overthrow and become Titans, and there are still ordinary people like me pretending to be devout, Okhema's vibe won't be too suffocating... especially if they claim to worship {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 说说你们冒牌信徒的活动。
t_en: Tell me about your activities as imposter believers.

t_ch: 怎么，看来你感兴趣？虽说最近活动经费有点紧张，没法直接邀请你参加…但和你描述一番圣城生活的另一面，我很有兴致！
t_en: Curious? Well, we've been tight on funds lately, so I can't really invite you... but if you want to learn more about the other side of life in this holy city, I'd love to spill the beans!

t_ch: 问问他们怎么聚会。
t_en: Ask about how they have gatherings.

t_ch: 其实还挺简单的。只要在传信石版的辩论场上匿名留言，「某某时刻，有谁要一起去祭拜塞纳托斯」，自然就有心领神会的家伙加入进来。
t_en: Well, it's pretty straightforward. We just post an anonymous message on the teleslate's Forum. "Anyone wants to worship Thanatos together at some Hour?" Insiders who understand what it means will naturally join.

t_ch: 至于具体的「祭拜」形式，那当然完全自由…唱歌跳舞吟诗作画——毫无美感也没有任何关系，只要自己开心就行！毕竟{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}对谁都一视同仁嘛。
t_en: When it comes to how we "worship," it's totally up to the individual... Singing, dancing, chanting, painting... even if your work is ugly. It's all about you having fun! After all, {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} treats everyone equally.

t_ch: 当然，这种聚会多少还是见不得人…再怎么说{RUBY_B#公认的黑潮元凶}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}也不是什么良善的泰坦，只不过它和它真正的信徒对一切都毫不在意罢了。
t_en: But of course, these gatherings can't exactly be held in broad daylight... After all, {RUBY_B#the black tide's assumed cause}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} isn't exactly a friendly Titan. It's just that they and their true disciples couldn't care less about anything.

t_ch: 实话说，上次我们的集会就被遐蝶小姐劝阻了，说奥赫玛再怎么样也是一座圣城，不可以这样无事生非、小题大作——她人虽然温柔，但那种生人勿近的感觉还是挺可怕的！
t_en: To be honest, Castorice dissuaded us at our previous gatherings, saying that Okhema is still a holy city no matter what, and we can't stir up drama like this. While she's gentle most of the time, she can be intimidating with how aloof she is at times!

t_ch: 问问他们有没有组织。
t_en: Ask about how their organization is.

t_ch: 组织？一群享乐主义的{RUBY_B#唯物论}泰坦自然论{RUBY_E#}者怎么可能有什么组织。我们借来塞纳托斯的名号，不就是不想参加那些恼人的迷信仪式？
t_en: Organization? Oh, come on, how could a bunch of hedonistic {RUBY_B#materialists}Titan naturalists{RUBY_E#} have an organization? We borrowed Thanatos' name because we wanted to avoid those pesky superstitious rites.

t_ch: 你要知道，膜拜泰坦的人虽多，但真的像祭司那样可以借用泰坦力量的人，其实少之又少。
t_en: While many folks worship the Titans, only a handful can actually harness the Titan's powers as the priests do.

t_ch: 大部分人其实不能因为参与仪式获得什么泰坦的恩惠，但他们就是觉得不这么做心里就不平衡、生活就没法宁静——你说可笑不可笑？
t_en: Most people don't really get any Titan blessings from participating in these ceremonies, but they won't find inner peace and won't feel right without worshipping. Isn't that ironic?

t_ch: 真正的祭司，比如缇宝大人她们，那我是相当敬重的，也认可他们是人们的组织者——我只是觉得，这样就够了，节外生枝搞迷信实在是累人累己。
t_en: I have respect for genuine priests like lady Tribbie and the others, and I acknowledge them as leaders of humanity — but that's enough for me. All that extra superstitious stuff is so exhausting.

t_ch: 问问世界的前途。
t_en: Ask about the future of the world.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#适者登神假说}演化神秘学{RUBY_E#}告诉我们——毁灭，然后新生！
t_en: {RUBY_B#The Apotheosis of the Fittest Hypothesis}Evoccultism{RUBY_E#} tells us that — We will be destroyed, then reborn!

t_ch: …开玩笑的。虽然上面的人都对世界被他们拯救之后究竟会如何所言甚少，但在我看来，这本身就是一种答案。
t_en: ...Just kidding. The higher-ups rarely reveal anything about what will happen after they save the world, but for me, that lack of an answer is already an answer in itself.

t_ch: 也就是说——没人知道世界的前途究竟会如何。即使黄金裔们也是如此。
t_en: In other words, no one really knows what's in store for this world in the future, not even the Chrysos Heirs.

t_ch: 照理说，这应该是一种不负责任的行为…但我们的确生活在一个预言真实存在的世界。即便是那些被奉若神明的泰坦，也无力改变预言——这也就是为什么，我觉得他们没资格高高在上。
t_en: To be honest, this isn't being responsible... but we live in a world where prophecies genuinely exist. Even the Titans, who are considered gods, are powerless to change prophecies. That's why I don't think they have the right to be all high and mighty.

t_ch: 从预先知道结果、但完全不知道应该怎样努力的角度来说，黄金裔们已经做得很好了。有些人对他们的苛责才是毫无道理。
t_en: Given that the Chrysos Heirs know the outcome but have no idea about how to get there, I think they are already doing a very good job. Some of the criticism they face is just plain unfair.

t_ch: 你别看他们说我愤世嫉俗，我可从来不说黄金裔们的坏话…呃，至少不会说阿格莱雅、遐蝶、还有缇宝那几位的坏话！
t_en: You might see people calling me cynical, but I've never badmouthed the Chrysos Heirs... Well, at least not Aglaea, Castorice, or Tribbie!

t_ch: （我想问的差不多了。）
t_en: (That's pretty much all I wanted to ask.)

t_ch: 今天忙，下次再聊。
t_en: I've got some business to attend to today. Catch you later.

t_ch: 忙一点好哇！我们这些人可能就是太闲了，所以才会庸人自扰——谁知道呢？
t_en: Business? Good. Staying busy is good. Being idle can often lead to self-inflicted trouble. Well, who knows?

t_ch: 这是一只普通的箩筐。如你所见，和垃圾桶没有任何相似之处。
t_en: What you see before you is simply an ordinary basket. As you can see, it bears no resemblance to a trashcan.

t_ch: 但为何…在众多箩筐之中，只有它吸引到了你的注意？
t_en: However, among all the baskets... why does this particular one capture your attention?

t_ch: 缘…妙不可言……
t_en: This... this must be fate...

t_ch: 它有着一颗垃圾桶的心！
t_en: This basket has the soul of a trashcan!

t_ch: 按F进行刺杀。
t_en: Press "F" to assassinate.

t_ch: 能看出这只箩筐并非等闲之辈，这一点值得赞许。
t_en: It's commendable that you recognize this is no ordinary basket.

t_ch: 不过，没礼貌的家伙——这是私人住宅，风能进、雨能进，唯独没有被邀请的国王不能进。懂？
t_en: However, this is my private domain, impolite one. Rain, wind, and sunshine are guests embraced, but not uninvited kings. You understand?

t_ch: 不懂。
t_en: Not really.

t_ch: 本大侠也是一种自然现象。
t_en: But I'm part of nature, too.

t_ch: 云石市集什么时候成私人住宅了？
t_en: Since when did Marmoreal Market become a personal property?

t_ch: …嗯？是我没说清楚吗——这只箩筐是我的家，没有我的许可，你不能乱翻它。这下明白了吗？
t_en: ...Hmm, did I not make it clear enough? This basket is my home, and you keep your hands off and don't dig through it unless I say otherwise. Got it now?

t_ch: 金色的{RUBY_B#浪漫之泰坦}墨涅塔{RUBY_E#}或许嫌贫爱富，但漆黑的{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}会平等地叩响所有房门。
t_en: The golden {RUBY_B#Romance Titan}Mnestia{RUBY_E#} might fancy the rich over the poor, but the dark {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} knocks on every door equally.

t_ch: 偷偷告诉你——我在这里给它们留了一只只有{RUBY_B#神}泰坦{RUBY_E#}才能看见的门铃。
t_en: Just between you and me, I've set up a secret doorbell for them, visible only to the {RUBY_B#gods}Titans{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 说说你的故事。
t_en: Tell me more about yourself.

t_ch: 那你可能要失望了，我并没有什么值得讲述的过去。无非是在元老院上班的爷爷遭遇了一场政治刺杀，结果全家只有我幸存了下来。
t_en: Then I'm sorry to disappoint. My background is pretty ordinary, except for the fact that my family was targeted in an assassination due to my grandpa's position in the Council of Elders, leaving me as the sole survivor.

t_ch: 别误会——我不缺钱。事实上，我们家在{RUBY_B#上面}黎明云崖{RUBY_E#}的庄园一切如旧；而且，通过这几年的教育，那些仆人也学会了不去关心他们的主人身在何方。
t_en: Don't get me wrong: I'm not hurting for money. In fact, our family's mansion in the {RUBY_B#up above}Dawncloud{RUBY_E#} hasn't changed. Over the years, our servants have also learned to not care about where their masters may be.

t_ch: 总之，多亏了这是个承认遗产合法性的城邦…在「再创世」到来之前，只要奥赫玛还没有在黑潮中陷落，我就能无拘无束地生活下去。想睡就睡，想吃就吃，想晒太阳就晒太阳——多好。
t_en: Anyway, thanks to this city's acceptance of legal inheritance... I can lead a carefree life until the Era Nova, as long as Okhema remains steadfast against the black tide. I sleep, eat, and soak up the sun whenever I please... What a bliss.

t_ch: 当然，如果你只是想听我随便聊点什么…那我们不妨把话题更明确一些？你是想知道{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}、{RUBY_B#浪漫之泰坦}墨涅塔{RUBY_E#}、还是元老院的故事？
t_en: But of course, if you're up for some casual chat... How about we pick a more specific topic? Interested in tales about {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}, {RUBY_B#Romance Titan}Mnestia{RUBY_E#}, or the Council of Elders?

t_ch: 说说塞纳托斯的故事。
t_en: Tell me more about Thanatos.

t_ch: 我没有办法知道真正的塞纳托斯是什么样，但我可以和你说说我知道的塞纳托斯。
t_en: Well, I may not know what Thanatos is truly like, but I can share how I see Thanatos.

t_ch: 对于那些笃信泰坦的人来说，{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}无疑是最可怕的神明。人们说祂催生黑潮，要把毁灭带给{RUBY_B#世界}翁法罗斯{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: For devout disciples of the Titans, the {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} is definitely a horrifying deity. They believe they are the mastermind behind the black tide, destined to bring catastrophe upon {RUBY_B#the world}Amphoreus{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 但我要说，塞纳托斯实际上是一个好人——这家伙默默地承受了世人所讨厌的一切，还为那些不容于世的人们提供了绝佳的保护伞。
t_en: But in my eyes, Thanatos is actually a good person. That Titan quietly endures all the hatred of the world and even offers a sanctuary for those ostracized by society.

t_ch: 这么说吧…如果你在奥赫玛听谁说自己笃信塞纳托斯，那么有很大的可能，这人只是不愿对泰坦顶礼膜拜，或者懒得参加任何祈求赐福的仪式。
t_en: Listen... If someone in Okhema declares themselves a follower of Thanatos, it's likely because they just prefer not to worship the Titans or partake in any rituals that beg for blessings.

t_ch: 毕竟，漆黑的{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}可不需要任何人顶礼膜拜，也不会对任何命不该绝的人赐予祝福嘛。
t_en: After all, the pitch-black {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} never needs adoration, and won't bestow blessings on anyone whose life is not meant to be over.

t_ch: 说说墨涅塔的故事。
t_en: Tell me more about Mnestia.

t_ch: 我很想说，{RUBY_B#爱与美的泰坦}墨涅塔{RUBY_E#}其实就是阿格莱雅姐姐的样子…但那是不对的。
t_en: I'd love to say that sister Aglaea looks just like {RUBY_B#Titan of Love and Beauty}Mnestia{RUBY_E#}... but that's not the case.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅姐姐不是那位泰坦的一个注脚，黄金裔们，也应该都有自己的理想和追求。
t_en: Sister Aglaea isn't just a side note for that Titan, and the same goes for the other Chrysos Heirs. I believe each of them treads their unique path with their own inspirations.

t_ch: 至于墨涅塔嘛…据我所知，那家伙更像是一位手忙脚乱的老妇人，从纷争世到幻灭世，眼睁睁地看着自己手中的绳结越来越没有办法解开。
t_en: As for Mnestia... from what I know, that goddess is more like a clumsy old lady. She juggled from Era Bellica to Era Erasa, watching helplessly as the knot in her hands became harder to unravel.

t_ch: 留给{RUBY_B#旧世界}翁法罗斯{RUBY_E#}的时间不多了…很多人都悄悄地这么说。
t_en: Destiny hasn't got much time left for {RUBY_B#the Old World}Amphoreus{RUBY_E#}... That's what many whisper in the shadows.

t_ch: 说说元老院的故事。
t_en: Tell me more about the Council of Elders.

t_ch: 元老院的故事很简单——那里是一群死人生活的地方。或许有人觉得元老院的存在自有其必要…但我多少算个受害者，显然不可能对你说出这样的话。
t_en: It's rather straightforward: The Council of Elders is a place teeming with the living dead. Some may argue the Council of Elders serves its intended functions... but as a victim, that's not the tale I would tell.

t_ch: 当我因为处理一些事情必须回那边的时候…哪怕天光永远大亮，哪怕周遭如闹市一般喧嚣，我还是提着灯也找不见哪怕一个真正的人。
t_en: When I had to return there for some business... even amidst the bustling streets, in broad daylight, or when I had a lamp in my hand... I didn't see a living soul.

t_ch: 当然，如果刻薄一点…整个{RUBY_B#世界}翁法罗斯{RUBY_E#}恐怕也没有几个人算是真正地活着。只是…黄金裔们还在这里。所以我不会这么去说。
t_en: To be a bit more strict... there might be only a handful truly alive in {RUBY_B#the entire world}Amphoreus{RUBY_E#}. However... the Chrysos Heirs are here, so I'll hold my tongue on that.

t_ch: 或者这么说吧——翁法罗斯或许真的在被一些我们尚不清楚的秘密支配…但了解那些秘密的人肯定不在元老院里。
t_en: Or maybe, let's put it this way: Amphoreus might actually be under the control of enigmatic forces beyond our grasp... But those who hold such knowledge certainly aren't seated in the Council of Elders.

t_ch: 他们只是自以为自己很了解罢了…还不如塞纳托斯或者某位自封的演化神秘学家来得可爱。
t_en: They just believe they know what's going on... They're even less endearing than Thanatos or that self-proclaimed Evoccultist.

t_ch: （我想问的差不多了。）
t_en: (That's pretty much all I wanted to ask.)

t_ch: 打扰了，再见。
t_en: See you next time.

t_ch: 再见，路过的{RUBY_B#一盏灯}有缘人{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: Farewell, {RUBY_B#bright lamp}destined one{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: …你好。欢迎来到全世界最大的浴场的图书室。理论上你可以从这里借阅到任何一本在奥赫玛出版的书籍——只要不介意填写调书单之后等待几天。
t_en: ...Greetings. Welcome to the library of the largest bath in the world. Theoretically, you can access any book published in Okhema, as long as you don't mind waiting a few days after filling out a book-transfer form.

t_ch: 你是这里的管理员？
t_en: You're an administrator here?

t_ch: 算是吧，不过和我一样做这份兼职的外地人还有很多。
t_en: Yeah, although many outlanders like me are working here as part-time administrators.

t_ch: 听上去你似乎不满意自己的工作。
t_en: Sounds like you're not too excited about your job.

t_ch: 那到不至于。逃难过来打一份临时工而已，没有什么工作热情也很正常吧？
t_en: Well, I'm here as a refugee, and this job is just to make ends meet temporarily. So it's natural I'm not too excited about it, right?

t_ch: …自我介绍一下吧，我的名字是阿波罗尼娅，来奥赫玛避难之前，曾经在一个被黑潮吞没的小城邦里做数学和天文学的研究。
t_en: ...Let me introduce myself. I'm Apollonia. I used to research math and astronomy in a small city that was later overrun by the black tide.

t_ch: 你可能也知道，天文学在奥赫玛属于禁忌，只有树庭的少数学者可以秘密研究。很不幸，最近逃难来的人实在有点多，他们那里也是僧多粥少，只好把我推荐来浴场工作。
t_en: As you might be aware, astronomy research is prohibited in Okhema, and only a few scholars in the Grove secretly manage to sneak a peek. With a flood of recent refugees, they don't have enough work for specialists like me and had to recommend me to work here in this bath.

t_ch: …当然了，也可能是因为给我写推荐信的那位教授人缘不好。
t_en: ...Or maybe because the professor who wrote my letter of recommendation wasn't so popular.

t_ch: 俗话说「开卷有益」，坚持用碎片时间来看书，哪怕一天只能看几页，一年下来还是能收获很多。
t_en: Reading is nourishment for the soul. Even a few pages each day can accumulate over a year, and you'll learn a great deal.

t_ch: 要是有什么能全职看书的工作，好像也不错啊。
t_en: Wouldn't it be a dream to read books all day for a living?

t_ch: 要不，说说你的研究吧。
t_en: How about telling me more about your research?

t_ch: 咦？你对研究感兴趣？真的？你要是唠这个，我可不困了啊。
t_en: Oh, really? You're interested in my research? Well, in that case, I'm all fired up.

t_ch: 聊聊在上一个城邦的研究。
t_en: Tell me more about your research from your previous city.

t_ch: 哦？你也对{RUBY_B#圆锥曲线}艾格勒曲线{RUBY_E#}感兴趣？我写过八卷本的《艾格勒曲线论》，只可惜原始稿件都已经被黑潮吞进肚子里了。
t_en: Oh? You're interested in {RUBY_B#Conic Section}Aquila Curves{RUBY_E#} too? I penned a comprehensive book called On Aquila Curves spanning eight volumes. It's a shame my manuscripts were lost in the black tide.

t_ch: 不过没关系，我的脑子还在——只要再花一年的时间，我可以把那本书里的所有命题和证明都重新写出来…说不定还能比以前做得更好。
t_en: But no worries, I recall all the details. Give me another year, and I'll rewrite all the propositions and methods to prove them... maybe I'll even do better than before!

t_ch: 只是在奥赫玛，天文学…你懂的。虽说《艾格勒曲线论》本身是纯数学的著作，但顶着这个名号，恐怕没什么人愿意和它扯上关系，更不要说资助我出版了。
t_en: It's just that... astronomy in Okhema... you know how it is. While On Aquila Curves is a book in pure mathematics, its title might deter people from exploring it, let alone securing a sponsor for publication.

t_ch: 不过没关系——我相信，只要黄金裔能解决预言中的问题，黑夜总有一天会重返天空，繁星将不再是任何人的禁忌。
t_en: But no worries... I believe that, as long as the Chrysos Heirs resolve the prophesied issues, the night sky will eventually return. By then, the starry sky will no longer be a forbidden topic anymore.

t_ch: 聊聊来奥赫玛之后的研究。
t_en: Tell me more about your research after arriving in Okhema.

t_ch: 上次我听说有不少人已经看腻了浴场里的装潢，就想着能不能从马赛克拼贴入手，帮助大家提供一些新的灵感。
t_en: I heard someone griping about how boring the bath's decor looks, and it got me thinking about coming up with some new mosaic designs to bring in some fresh aspirations.

t_ch: 我编了一套石板上的程序，设计算法寻找可以密铺镶嵌的五边形马赛克，总共找出来了<unbreak>15</unbreak>种——只可惜有建筑师后来和我说，五边形的地砖在生产成本上还是有点太超前了。
t_en: In the end, I designed a program on the tablet and wrote algorithms to find pentagon-shaped mosaics that could cover the floor smoothly, and I discovered <unbreak>15</unbreak> different patterns. But when I mentioned the idea to an architect, they warned me that mass-producing pentagonal tiles might be too costly.

t_ch: 唉，或许我应该把这个点子推销给云石市集里的哪位赚钱高手，拿去设计点拼图玩具什么的？不过隔行如隔山，这种主意可能在人家那里也不是什么好点子。
t_en: Perhaps I should share this idea with some smart vendor at the Marmoreal Market to turn it into producing jigsaws or puzzle toys? But then again, toy design is not my forte, and this whole concept might not click with them.

t_ch: 聊聊树庭。
t_en: Tell me more about the Grove.

t_ch: 啊…虽然同为研究者，但我对树庭还真没什么了解。之前说过，有位人缘不好的教授曾经帮我给树庭写了封推荐信——
t_en: Well... despite being a researcher, I'm not well-versed in the Grove. As I mentioned, a not-so-popular professor wrote a recommendation letter for me...

t_ch: 「如果有人看见复杂的论述就认为对方的头脑缺乏条理，我力荐这样的人来读一读阿波罗尼娅的这本《艾格勒曲线论》。」
t_en: "If individuals tend to presume a lack of mental acuity on the part of the author when faced with intricate explanations, I strongly suggest delving into Apollonia's work — On Aquila Curves."

t_ch: 「此人将会发现，没有哪个有才智的人（不论是多么有天分的），可以将这些知识表述成仅需一目十行的阅读就可以明白的方式。」
t_en: "Through this exploration, one will find that no intellectual (regardless of their talent) can articulate such intricate concepts in a manner that can be understood through skim-reading."

t_ch: ——啊。该不会，这也是他不受其他人欢迎的一个方面？
t_en: ...Hmmm, could that style have contributed to his lack of popularity?

t_ch: （我想问的差不多了。）
t_en: (That's pretty much all I wanted to ask.)

t_ch: 打工辛苦。
t_en: Working for a salary is no walk in the park.

t_ch: 彼此彼此！能看得出来，你好像也挺不容易的。有空再聊~
t_en: Indeed! I have a feeling that you have had a hard time as well. Talk to you later!

t_ch: 你好，陌生的孩子。世界苦难深重，人生不如意之事也十有八九。但没关系，在这座美丽的圣城里，我们都可以获得片刻的安宁。
t_en: Greetings, unfamiliar child. The world is full of agony, and life is brimming with misery and mishaps. However, don't fret about it. We can all obtain a momentary peace here in this beautiful holy city.

t_ch: 老身名为塔瓦纳安娜，来自遥远东方的哈图西里城邦。虽说在黑潮之前，我的家乡也曾像花园一样美丽，但赞美{RUBY_B#大地之泰坦}磐岩之脊{RUBY_E#}——果然还是{RUBY_B#奥赫玛}刻法勒庇护下的圣城{RUBY_E#}举世无双。
t_en: My name is Tavananna, hailing all the way from Hattusili in the far east. While my homeland was a paradise before the black tide — praise the {RUBY_B#Earth Titan}Pillar of Stone{RUBY_E#} — its charm would pale in comparison to the splendor of {RUBY_B#Okhema}the holy city under Kephale's wings{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 陌生的孩子，你莫非是初来乍到？无论是这空中的花园、还是隔壁的大浴场，它们都是我们这些闲暇市民，消磨时光的绝佳去处。
t_en: Are you new around here, unfamiliar child? Whether it's this hanging gardens or the grand bath nearby, these are the best spots for us citizens to while away the hours when we are idle.

t_ch: 我倒也没有那么闲。
t_en: I'm not really that idle.

t_ch: 哎呀，忙碌的年轻人，要注意劳逸结合哦。早睡早起、按时吃饭、少喝凉水，啊——遇到不顺心的事就先停下来深吸一口气，慢就是快，磨刀可不误砍柴工啊。
t_en: Ah, a busy bee, I see, but remember to balance work and play. Early to bed, early to rise, eat well, and skip those icy drinks... When trouble comes knocking, take a breather, because rushing leads to blunders.

t_ch: 你好像并不在意世界的危机。
t_en: Looks like you're not too bothered about all the calamity.

t_ch: …嗐，你可别被有些咋咋呼呼的年轻人给骗了。总说什么世界末日之类的胡话——哪有的事！这传信石版上天天都在宣讲，元老院和黄金裔的团结坚如磐石，那还能有什么可怕的事？
t_en: ...Hah, don't let those rowdy youngsters get under your skin. They're making a fair share of doomsayers — it's all just noise! Have you seen what the government posts on teleslates every day? With the Council of Elders and the Chrysos Heirs joining forces, what could possibly go wrong?

t_ch: 孩子啊，你千万别嫌我们这种老人唠叨。来来来，相逢就是缘…这个见面红包，孩子你一定要收下，啊？
t_en: Child, please don't mind us oldies nagging. It's fate that's brought us together. Here... this little "greeting gift" is for you. Take it, my child.

t_ch: 别客气，老身也没有什么别的爱好，就喜欢看年轻人开开心心的样子。看着你们年轻人乐呵，老身自己也就一样乐呵，多好的事，哈哈。
t_en: You're welcome. I don't have many hobbies besides watching you youngsters enjoy yourselves. Your happiness is mine, so keep that positive vibe going, alright?

t_ch: 人们都说，如果{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}为你关上了一扇门，那么它一定还会为你打开一扇窗。
t_en: People say: If the {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#} closes a door to you, you can be certain that they have opened a window somewhere.

t_ch: 就算长棍面包在{RUBY_B#大地之泰坦}吉奥里亚{RUBY_E#}沉睡之时踪迹难寻，{RUBY_B#海洋之泰坦}法吉娜{RUBY_E#}也一定会为我们颁赐甜蜜的戚风蛋糕。
t_en: Even though baguettes are hard to find while the {RUBY_B#Earth Titan}Georios{RUBY_E#} slumbers, rest assured, because the benevolent {RUBY_B#Ocean Titan}Phagousa{RUBY_E#} will certainly bless us with their sweet chiffon cakes.

t_ch: 老奶奶，能和你打听点事吗？
t_en: May I ask you something, granny?

t_ch: 哎呀，那当然没有问题。虽说老身也是外地人，知道的东西绝没有那真言狮口多…但你放心，像我们这样年纪大的人，最不缺的反而就是时间。
t_en: No problem at all. While I may not be as wise as the Lion's Maw of Truth, and I'm an outlander myself... time is one thing us elders have in abundance.

t_ch: 打听奥赫玛的事
t_en: Ask about Okhema.

t_ch: 哦，你想打听这圣城里的事，对吧？照理来说应该推荐你几本书读，不过这年头，传信石版比书可好用多了。来，老身念几段{RUBY_B#公益广告}导善铭文{RUBY_E#}给你听听——
t_en: Oh, you're interested in this holy city? Books are great for learning about the city, but these days, teleslates make things so much easier. Here, let me read you a bit from the {RUBY_B#public service announcements}goodwill inscriptions{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 「欢迎来到刻法勒庇护的奥赫玛，远道的异乡人。这里是翁法罗斯的最后乐土，也是由黄金裔守护的永恒之城。」
t_en: "Outlanders from distant lands, welcome to Okhema, the sanctum under the watchful eye of Kephale. This is the Eternal City, shielded by the vigilant Chrysos Heirs, the last paradise in Amphoreus."

t_ch: 「四方的公民啊——欢迎你们来到这里，我们将张开双臂，和你们同享万世不变的祥和与安乐。」
t_en: "Welcome, citizens from all directions. Within these walls, we extend to you our open arms, sharing eternal peace and joy."

t_ch: 看，只要稍稍关注几个{RUBY_B#公众号}云上广场{RUBY_E#}，我们外地人也能对{RUBY_B#奥赫玛}刻法勒的圣城{RUBY_E#}了如指掌。
t_en: See? Just follow a few {RUBY_B#public accounts}Nebula Plazas{RUBY_E#}, and even us outlanders can get the lowdown on {RUBY_B#Okhema}Kephale's holy city{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 就连我这身衣服，也是在传信石版上，从大裁缝阿芙罗的店里定来的。这年头，真离不了传信石版呐。
t_en: I even ordered my dress from the great tailor Aphrea's store using my teleslate. These gadgets have become essential for our daily lives now.

t_ch: 打听外部城邦的事
t_en: Ask about other cities.

t_ch: 啊，外面的事吗？其实老身也知道，这年头除了{RUBY_B#奥赫玛}刻法勒的圣城{RUBY_E#}，四下里的确是乱得很。那个黑潮更了不得，哗地一下就淹过去，除了随身细软，那是什么也带不出来。
t_en: The outside world? Well, I know it's pretty chaotic out there everywhere except in {RUBY_B#Okhema}Kephale's holy city{RUBY_E#}, and that black tide is a real menace. In the blink of an eye, it can swallow a whole city, and you'll barely have enough time to run for your life.

t_ch: 不过你大可放心！只要来了{RUBY_B#奥赫玛}刻法勒的圣城{RUBY_E#}，有困难找元老院和黄金裔就好。你唯一的烦恼，只可能是无事可做，耐不住寂寞…哈哈。
t_en: But everything's safe in {RUBY_B#Okhema}Kephale's holy city{RUBY_E#}! If you ever run into trouble, just head to the Council of Elders or find the Chrysos Heirs. Your only trouble might be a bit of boredom... haha.

t_ch: 打听你的事
t_en: Ask about Tavananna.

t_ch: 老身的事？…嗐，老身能有什么故事，每天悠哉游哉，四世同堂幸福得很。
t_en: About me...? Oh, my life's simple, enjoying my leisure time and family moments.

t_ch: …只可惜老伴也好、孩子们也好，一个个都太过忙碌，没有一点闲下来的时间陪老身在花园里坐坐。
t_en: ...It's a shame my husband and kids are always tied up, too busy to share moments with this old lady.

t_ch: 哦——别往心里去，老身倒也没有非要拉着你坐下唠唠的意思。你忙你的，挺好、挺好！
t_en: No need to feel obligated, really. I'm not trying to guilt-trip you into sticking around. Just handle your business! No stress!

t_ch: （我想问的差不多了。）
t_en: (That's pretty much all I wanted to ask.)

t_ch: 不打扰你消磨时光。
t_en: I'll be out of your hair now.

t_ch: 哈哈，老身知道，年轻人都很忙。想做什么就做什么，挺好的！不过要是身心疲劳了，那就来花园里坐一坐，或者去泡泡温泉…可别把自己累着了，啊。
t_en: Aha, you youngsters are always on the move. Always doing whatever you want. Good! And hey, don't forget to take a break in the garden or a dip in the hot spring... Just don't push yourself too hard, okay?

t_ch: 可曾听闻圣山之上，最后的城邦\n不朽神明岿然背负，永恒的太阳
t_en: Have you heard of the last city, on the Holy Mountain high?\nWhere the immortal deity carries the sun across the eternal sky?

t_ch: 沉默中黎明未曾逝去\n凝滞里生命尚存希望
t_en: Dawn lingers, silent in its eloquent grace.\nHope shines bright amidst the world's stagnant embrace.

t_ch: （继续听）
t_en: (Keep listening.)

t_ch: 失乡人啊，你的前路应往何方？\n黑潮汹涌，混沌肆虐生死惶惶
t_en: Homeless, where should you find your way?\nIn black tide and despair, where shadows play.

t_ch: 流亡者啊，你的明日可有念想？\n众神沉寂，命理昏昧人伦癫狂
t_en: Exile, what dreams for days to come?\nAmidst silent gods, fate's whisper grows crazed and numb.

t_ch: 寻求那末世之中的乐土吧\n彼处尚存日光、清泉和丰壤
t_en: In the end times, seek the promised land.\nSunlight, clear waters, fertile soil, where peace will stand.

t_ch: 去往那仁慈神祇的庭园吧\n彼处仍留笑语、欢宴与佳酿
t_en: Within the garden of that merciful god, solace can be found.\nLaughter, feasts, and wine, joy in abundance unbound.

t_ch: 这首「最后的庭园」献给您——可爱的{F#女士}{M#先生}。
t_en: This poem, "The Last Veranda," is dedicated to you, adorable {F#lady}{M#mister}.

t_ch: （打断他）
t_en: (Interrupt him.)

t_ch: 这位可爱的{F#女士}{M#先生}，利波向您问好~
t_en: Greetings, adorable {F#lady}{M#mister}. Lipos is here, sending you warm regards!

t_ch: 唔…利波在您身上嗅到了广袤天空的味道：浅淡又遥远，但是如此的令人心驰神往。
t_en: Hmhm... Lipos senses the vast sky's aroma around you: So close, yet distant, and utterly enchanting.

t_ch: 感谢命运指引我们在此相遇，有什么想听的诗歌吗？利波愿意为您献上全部的收藏~
t_en: Thank fate for this moment we share. Any poem striking your fancy today? Lipos got a trove waiting just for you!

t_ch: 感谢命运指引我们在此相遇，有什么想听的诗歌吗？利波愿意为您献上全部的收藏~
t_en: Thank fate for this moment we share. Any poem striking your fancy today? Lipos got a trove waiting just for you!

t_ch: 你都知道什么诗歌？
t_en: What kinds of poetry are you familiar with?

t_ch: 您想听些什么？古老黄金裔间的情与爱、黄金战争末期的乱世、沿海百邦的崛起与没落…利波这里应有尽有！
t_en: What piques your interest? Love amidst the ancient Chrysos Heirs? The aftermath of the Chrysos War's chaos? Or perhaps the rise and fall of those hundred Seaside States...? Lipos is here to serve!

t_ch: 古老黄金裔的情与爱。
t_en: Love amidst the ancient Chrysos Heirs.

t_ch: 哈哈，这类的故事向来最受欢迎~那么这首《阿多尼娅之死》献给您——
t_en: Haha, a classic choice indeed! Here's a poem named Adonia's Death just for you...

t_ch: 「天生的绝伦美艳、蒙受诸泰坦垂怜，阿多尼娅的昳丽令万物黯然。」
t_en: "Beauty since the cradle, adored by the Titans' gaze. Adonia's brilliance eclipsed all in its blaze."

t_ch: 「墨涅塔的信徒甘在少女石榴裙下俯首，塞纳托斯的使者阴影般于她身侧常伴。」
t_en: "Mnestia's disciple bowed to the maiden's grace. While Thanatos' messenger lingered beside her, a solemn hem."

t_ch: 「信徒赠与诗歌、鲜花与昂贵的金绸；使者奉献美酒、石榴与守护之誓言。」
t_en: "Verses, blooms, treasures untold by the disciple laid. Wine, pomegranates, vows of protection by the messenger displayed."

t_ch: 「热烈的爱恋岂能辜负，真挚的情意安可亏欠——少女抉择不定，惴惴难安。」
t_en: "How should I respond to their love, so fervent and true? Adonia hesitated, turmoil in her heart anew."

t_ch: 「『为我求取泰坦之火吧，』终于，阿多尼娅如是说，『如此方可与我一生相伴。』」
t_en: "The one who brings the Titans' fire shall be my eternal mate. So the lady claimed, sealing their fate."

t_ch: 「黄金裔们逐火而去，日复一日、年复一年；远行者尚未归来，而黑潮近在眼前。」
t_en: "Chrysos Heirs departed on the quest's call. Years pass, none returned, as the black tide did fall."

t_ch: 「城邦覆没、众人流离 ；阿多尼娅恪守诺言，独留空城翘首以盼。」
t_en: "The city crumbled, its people scattered wide. Adonia waited, faithful, till the final tide."

t_ch: 「二人旅途未竟，仓惶归乡；却见万籁死寂，唯有黑潮肆意蔓延。」
t_en: "Returners, met with silence's deafening wail. Only the black tide's encroaching violence, a bitter tale."

t_ch: 「信徒悲泣三日自刎而死，血液生出红玫瑰，明媚娇艳。」
t_en: "The disciple wept for three days and took his life. Red roses sprung from his blood, vivid and rife."

t_ch: 「使者自此以往沉默不言，昼夜徘徊冥河岸，可怖疯癫。」
t_en: "Messenger fell silent, lost in Styx's dark embrace. Madness claimed him, a forlorn, unsettled place."

t_ch: 黄金战争末期的乱世。
t_en: The aftermath of the Chrysos War's chaos.

t_ch: 唔…那段历史可是相当残酷而混乱呢。那么这首《纷争事纪》献给您——
t_en: Ah, those were tumultuous times. Enjoy this poem titled Era of Strifes dedicated to you...

t_ch: 「故事发生在黄金暗淡的世纪，神的纷争让人的世界分崩离析：」
t_en: "In a somber age when golden brilliance waned. When divine clashes shook the earthly plane."

t_ch: 「古老的预言无人相信，贪婪和欲念铸就野心；人群中暗流涌动，城邦间烽火燃起。」
t_en: "Ancient prophecies dismissed, greed's flames ignited. Conspiracies breed, city-states engage in the fight."

t_ch: 「且看黄金裔们如何堕落，被无尽的欲望吞噬折磨；鲜血染红了河流与大地，战斗的呼喊声仍未止息。」
t_en: "Chrysos Heirs, tormented by their insatiable yearning. Bloodied rivers, soil tainted, battle cries echoing, burning."

t_ch: 「直到水源枯干、树木凋敝，天空晦暗、土地贫瘠——无情的战争还在继续，只因满腔欲念未曾止息。」
t_en: "Waters dry, trees fade, skies weep, lands stripped bare. Unyielding war rages on, fueled by boundless desire."

t_ch: 「民众颤抖不已，心中充满恐惧；军队蹒跚而行，践踏一切正义。」
t_en: "In trembling hearts, fear takes root deep and near. Armies advancing, justice a distant dream. World fractured, crushed beneath the relentless stream."

t_ch: 「荒野支离，纷争的铁蹄粉碎明日的希冀；家园破碎，空洞的眼眶盈满冰冷的泪滴……」
t_en: "Homes razed, lifeless eyes shedding icy tears. In a shattered world where hope disappears..."

t_ch: 沿海百邦的崛起没落。
t_en: The rise and fall of the hundred Seaside States.

t_ch: 那些湮灭在历史洪流中的城邦啊…可真令人怀念向往。那么这首《黄金时代》献给您——
t_en: The cities lost in history's flow evoke such nostalgia... Please delight in this piece named The Golden Age...

t_ch: 「最初的人们聚集在刻法勒脚下，文明的种子便在翁法罗斯萌芽。请看——」
t_en: "At Kephale's feet, humanity's dawn unfurled. Civilization's roots in Amphoreus twirled."

t_ch: 「阿里斯蒂亚的岩石与海浪之间，绵延着大理石寺庙、海浪形喷泉，与宏伟的宫殿；」
t_en: "Aristia's shores graced by temples grand. Fountains like waves, palaces that stand."

t_ch: 「麦迪特瑞恩的沃土与和煦日光，滋育了饱满的橄榄、黄金的蜜果、与广阔葡萄园。」
t_en: "Mediterrana's embrace, earth rich and warm. Nurturing olives, fruits, vineyards in charm."

t_ch: 「学者与智者在欧瑞尼亚播撒知识与智慧之种；文学与艺术在米里奥斯结出的果实分外甘甜。」
t_en: "Aurelia's scholars sow wisdom's seed. Literature and art in Milios freed."

t_ch: 「耶利卡的港口船只往来不息；皮利亚的驼兽络绎无闲。」
t_en: "Jericha's ports, alive with ships in array. Pyria's domain, filled with camelbeasts at bay."

t_ch: 「奥凯纳斯的盛会追求肉体极限，竞技者挥汗如雨，喝彩声震天；」
t_en: "Okeanas' festivals, mortals push their might. Athletes sweat, cheered in contests and fights."

t_ch: 「阿卡索斯的祭典携来繁华飨宴，游行者载歌载舞，纵情须狂欢。」
t_en: "Icatus' celebrations, a blaze of delight. Parades of song, dance, feasting in the night."

t_ch: 「呜呼——诸多奇观美景、早湮灭历史洪流之间，唯余叹惋。」
t_en: "Ah... so much beauty, lost to time's cruel hand. A tale of sorrow, slipping like sand."

t_ch: 暂时不想听了。
t_en: I've had my fill already.

t_ch: 没关系，利波就在这，随时等候着您的到来~
t_en: No worries. Lipos is here, patiently awaiting your return.

t_ch: 停停停、下次一定。
t_en: Wait, wait. Let's do it next time.

t_ch: 没关系，利波就在这，随时等候着您的到来~
t_en: No worries. Lipos is here, patiently awaiting your return.

t_ch: 你有自己的作品吗？
t_en: Do you write your own poems, too?

t_ch: 那当然啦，利波还出版过自己的诗集呢！不仅如此，承蒙恩师的教导，利波还会用泰坦语写诗呢！这可不是谁都能做到的哦~
t_en: Certainly! I've even penned my own collection of poems. Under my mentor's tutelage, I even ventured into crafting poetry in Titan language! Quite the extraordinary feat, if I may say so myself!

t_ch: 啊，说起来…您与黄金裔英雄们的故事着实波澜壮阔，所以利波就擅自将它们记录下来啦。请来听听利波为您写的诗吧，希望您能喜欢——
t_en: Ah, speaking of that... your epic journey with the Chrysos Heirs has inspired me. Here's a poem I've composed just for you. May it resonate deeply with you...

t_ch: 《逐火者之歌》
t_en: Epic of the Flame-Chaser

t_ch: 穿越长夜之幕，临世的闪耀星辰
t_en: In the night's embrace, a star so bright. World's shimmering beacon, a radiant light.

t_ch: 蒙受命运降福，神秘的异乡旅人
t_en: Destined, veiled in mystery's sheen. An outlander in enigma's serene.

t_ch: 悬锋陷落，灾殃骤起，与英雄共赴——
t_en: Amidst rising plague and crumbling Kremnos. The outlander joins heroes, dispelling great woes.

t_ch: 扣响时空之扉、追忆往昔之影
t_en: To ring time's bells, recall days of yore. Hunting corrupted gold, lost to the core.

t_ch: 狩猎颓堕神明、还其荣耀安宁
t_en: Restoring former glory, the tale untold. In echoes of history, a new chapter unfolds.

t_ch: 暂时不想听了。
t_en: I've had my fill already.

t_ch: 没关系，利波就在这，随时等候着您的到来~
t_en: No worries. Lipos is here, patiently awaiting your return.

t_ch: 你好！
t_en: Hello!

t_ch: 我要入浴！
t_en: I want to take a bath!

t_ch: 有人吗？
t_en: Anyone there?

t_ch: 嘿，理一下我——
t_en: Hey, don't ignore me...

t_ch: 啊，对不起，客人，我走神了。您是…我听说过您的事，从异邦来的，协助黄金裔大人们的战士，对吧？
t_en: Ah, apologies, good guest. I was distracted. You are... I've heard about you before — the warrior from foreign lands who fights side by side with Chrysos Heirs, am I correct?

t_ch: 欢迎来到云石天宫，客人。阿格莱雅大人交代过我们，要将您与您的同伴当作贵宾来接待。
t_en: Welcome to Marmoreal Palace, good guest. Lady Aglaea has instructed us to receive you and your fine companions as esteemed guests.

t_ch: 有什么贵宾特权吗？
t_en: Any perks that come with that?

t_ch: 当然，阿格莱雅大人应该已经给您简单介绍过这里了，那请允许我说明一下——
t_en: Of course. Lady Aglaea must have told you about this place, but allow me to give you a thorough introduction...

t_ch: 可你刚刚无视我。
t_en: But you just ignored me.

t_ch: 实在抱歉，客人。刚刚我的思绪飘到了万径之门外面，绝非有意要无视您。
t_en: My sincerest apologies, good guest. My thoughts wandered out of the Gate of Infinity just now, I absolutely did not mean to ignore you.

t_ch: 不如说，如果您不介意，我希望多跟您聊聊，听您讲讲城外的故事。
t_en: Rather, if you don't mind, I would hope to converse further with you, and listen to your tales outside the city.

t_ch: 先不提这些，阿格莱雅大人应该给您简单介绍过这里了，那请允许我补充说明一下您拥有的权益。
t_en: These matters aside, Lady Aglaea should have briefed you about this place. Please allow me to elaborate on the privileges you possess.

t_ch: 您可以随意出入浴场、免费使用浴场的各项设施，同时，在需额外付费的项目中享有专属折扣，但一部分贩卖品不在此限。
t_en: You can enter and exit the bath anytime, use the bath's various facilities free of charge, and enjoy exclusive discounts on paid amenities, though this offer does not extend to some products.

t_ch: 这么好！
t_en: That's amazing!

t_ch: 让人们洗去脏污，获得安宁，是云石天宫创设的初衷，而协助守卫奥赫玛的战士们更加值得敬佩，所以提供对应的服务是我们应该做的。
t_en: Allowing people to wash off their taint and attain serenity — that's the original purpose of the Marmoreal Palace. The warriors who assisted in defending Okhema are even more worthy of commendation, so it is only natural that we provide the respective services.

t_ch: 怎么不是全免费？
t_en: Why isn't it all free?

t_ch: 很抱歉，工作人员需要养活自己…再者说，如果太乱来，也不符合塔兰顿的教诲。
t_en: Sincerest apologies, but the staff here also need to earn a living... Also, it does not reflect well on Talanton's teachings if a good intention goes astray.

t_ch: 此外图书室、池中餐厅等设施…嗯，这部分还是让浴场的其他同事们为您介绍的好。
t_en: As for the reading room, pool restaurant and other amenities... Hmm, I'd better leave their introductions to my bath coworkers.

t_ch: 我要说的就这些了，您可以入场逛逛。我会接着在这发呆，有需要就来找我吧。
t_en: This is all I have to say. You may go in and have a look around as you please. I will proceed to daydream here and snap back to reality if you need me.

t_ch: 又走神了？
t_en: Daydreaming again?

t_ch: 打扰一下——
t_en: Helloooo...

t_ch: 抱歉，入场请往…啊，是您啊，客人。很高兴再见到您，有什么需要吗？
t_en: Apologies, this way to enter... Ah, it is you, my good guest. Glad to see you again, how may I help you?

t_ch: 我是来聊天的。
t_en: I'm here to chat.

t_ch: 嗯，您看起来可真清闲，真让人羡慕…虽然我也差不多。
t_en: Mm, your apparent idleness is enviable... even if it is similar for me.

t_ch: 怎么每次见你都在发呆？
t_en: Why are you always in a daze when I see you?

t_ch: 因为瀑布。
t_en: Because of the waterfall.

t_ch: …我是不是，应该补充说明一下？
t_en: ...Should I... elaborate?

t_ch: 就像艺术家们说，欧洛尼斯所代表的永夜是最适合激发创作灵感的事物。而对于我而言，能够激发思考的是它——云石天宫的瀑布。
t_en: Just as the artists say, the eternal night that Oronyx symbolizes is a thing most suited to spark the inspiration of creation. And to me, the thing that stimulates my thoughts is this — Marmoreal Palace's waterfall.

t_ch: 听着哗啦啦的水声，很多理不清的思绪都会迎刃而解。代价就是经常被管理员念叨：「消极怠工，小心塞纳托斯上身」。
t_en: Myriad knotted thoughts will undo themselves when I listen to its constant splashing barrage. The only price I have to pay is the manager's constant yammering: "Keep slacking off and Thanatos shall take you away."

t_ch: 工作还顺利吗？
t_en: How's work?

t_ch: 还好，之前的袭击确实产生了一定影响，好在总体还算安稳。只是…战士或是卫兵之类的客人可能需要我们额外关注一下。
t_en: It's all right. The previous attack has had some effect, but everything else is fine. It's just... we need to be more watchful with guests who are warriors or guards in some capacity.

t_ch: 就我的观察来看，受了轻伤而前来泡澡疗愈的客人变得更多了，也有一些老熟客缺勤…希望他们平安无事。
t_en: From what I've noticed, there has been a rise in guests with minor injuries coming for a soak, as well as a downturn of regular customers... I hope they are all right.

t_ch: 但…我相信这只是暂时的。奥赫玛的居民一直都很坚强，未来也会是如此——愿刻法勒庇护我们。
t_en: But... I believe that this is temporary. Okhema's people have always been hardy, and so will the future — may Kephale protect us.

t_ch: 给我讲讲浴场吧。
t_en: Tell me about the baths.

t_ch: 嗯，要我为阿格莱雅大人做补充，实在是有些惶恐…那我就给您讲些只有我们工作人员才知道的事好了。
t_en: Mm, it somewhat terrifies me that I am to elaborate on Lady Aglaea's words... I shall tell you about matters that only staff are privy to then.

t_ch: 云石天宫的历史能追溯到几千个光历年前——对外是这么说的，实际上应该没有这么久。但另一方面，这反倒更加凸显了建造者们的才华。
t_en: We claim that the Marmoreal Palace's history dates back to several thousand Light Calendar years, but its history is technically shorter than that. The fact that it's younger than as claimed has only highlighted the talent of its builders.

t_ch: 再说浴池本身，除了中央浴池外，其他池子里并不都是普通的水，还混了一些对身体好的添加物，比如浴盐、牛奶、还有法吉娜的秘酿等等。
t_en: As for the baths themselves, apart from the central pool, the water in the other pools is no ordinary water, but mixed with substances that nourish the body, such as bath salts, milk, and Phagousa's ambrosia, among other things.

t_ch: 它们各自具有不同的功效，您可以通过香气来辨别。另外…有人说直接饮用可以获得更好的效果，但我个人非常不建议您这么做。
t_en: They all offer different effects, and you can tell the difference from their aroma. Also... some people say that imbibing the bath water directly yields even more effective results, but I strongly suggest against that.

t_ch: 没事，不打扰你了。
t_en: No worries, I won't bother you then.

t_ch: 不会，愿您在云石天宫里洗去疲惫，客人。
t_en: You're not. May your fatigue be washed away in Marmoreal Palace, good guest.

t_ch: 没事，不打扰你了。
t_en: No worries, I won't bother you then.

t_ch: 不会，愿您在云石天宫里洗去疲惫，客人。
t_en: You're not. May your fatigue be washed away in Marmoreal Palace, good guest.

t_ch: 我想买点什么。
t_en: I want to buy something.

t_ch: 没问题，不过城内最近刚刚经历了变故，商品种类还不够齐全，我看看…目前在列的有这些，如果您感兴趣，我可以详细介绍一下。
t_en: That's no problem, but the city has recently undergone some changes, and we have a limited selection of merchandise. Let's see... We currently have these for sale. I can introduce them in detail if you're interested.

t_ch: 「光历会籍」
t_en: Light Calendar Membership

t_ch: 客人，我们非常感激您的心意，但入会两次实在是对您的财富太不健康了。还是请看看别的吧。
t_en: Good guest, we are extremely grateful, but joining the membership twice will be far too harmful to your wealth. Please browse something else.

t_ch: 如果您有意成为浴场的常客，那么办个付费会籍绝对是不二之选。
t_en: If you intend to be a regular at our baths, a membership is surely the wisest choice.

t_ch: 可我的贵宾特权…
t_en: But my VIP privileges...

t_ch: 入场不是免费吗？
t_en: Isn't it free to enter the baths?

t_ch: 啊，请不要误会，会籍并不能带来折扣或是积分之类的东西。硬要说的话，这份支出类似于献给法吉娜的礼物——
t_en: Ah, please do not misunderstand, membership does not include discounts or membership points. Simply put, this payment is akin to a gift for Phagousa...

t_ch: …实话实说，其实云石天宫的浴场，一开始并不是免费的，采用的是后付费制。设计者或许有他的道理，但作为客人而言，会有种受到了欺骗的感觉。
t_en: ...Truth be told, Marmoreal Palace's bath was not meant to be free in the beginning — we employed the pay-as-you-go policy. The designer may have their reasons behind this but it felt more like a scam to our guests when they were asked to pay.

t_ch: 后来就再也没人提起这件事了，但我们保留了付费的渠道——这就是这个会籍的实际意义。所以在入会之后，您会获得来自浴场全体工作人员的敬意！
t_en: No one ever brought this matter up later on, but we still kept the payment channel — this is the true meaning of the membership. Which is why you will have the respect of all bath staff if you join our membership!

t_ch: 就这样？
t_en: So that's what it is, huh?

t_ch: 就这样。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 没别的了？
t_en: That's it?

t_ch: 没别的了。
t_en: That's it.

t_ch: 请问…您意下如何？
t_en: If I may... Would you like to join our membership?

t_ch: 我入会！
t_en: I'll join up!

t_ch: …说实话，我没想到您真的会掏钱。啊，请不要误会，我没有贬低您的意思，只是单纯因为太久没人买过了。
t_en: ...Honestly, I didn't expect you'd really do this. Ah, please do not misunderstand, I had no intention of disparaging you, it was simply because it's been ages since someone joined our membership.

t_ch: 我这就将您的慷慨付出发布在云石天宫的辩论场里。
t_en: I will spread word of your generosity across Marmoreal Palace's debate forum.

t_ch: 过了一阵……
t_en: Later...

t_ch: 「认可」数已经超过了三万？还有其他客人提出想要入会……
t_en: Approval numbers have exceeded <unbreak>30,000</unbreak>? And there are other guests who wish to join the membership...

t_ch: 我不知道该说什么好了，客人，您是浴场的英雄。事已至此，若是不给您什么回报，塔兰顿定会判决我们忘恩负义…就让我为您念一段浴场的祷词吧。
t_en: I don't know what to say, good guest. You are a hero of the bath. As it stands, if we don't present you a fair reward, Talanton will surely castigate us for our ingratitude... Allow me to chant a bath prayer for you then.

t_ch: 「水中舞动的人，痛饮秘酿的人，为法吉娜与它的追随者欢呼。在海洋上，在风暴中，我们与无常做伴，笑容如影随形。」
t_en: "They who sway in water, who savor secret brew, rejoice in Phagousa and their devotee. Over the oceans, within the tempests, we accept transience as constant company, yet among us happiness prevails wondrously."

t_ch: 您的名字会永远记录在云石天宫的历史上——愿法吉娜庇佑您，客人。
t_en: Your name will forever be remembered in Marmoreal Palace's annals — may Phagousa protect you, good guest.

t_ch: 算了算了。
t_en: Forget it.

t_ch: 嗯，如此不合算的买卖，您会拒绝也没办法，还请不要放在心上。看看其他的吗？
t_en: Mm, it is only natural that you refuse such a financial sham. Please do not take it to heart. Will you take a look at the others?

t_ch: 「旧诗浴盐」
t_en: Classical Bath Salts

t_ch: 很抱歉，客人，上次那件已经是我们最后的存货了，到货时我会再通知您。
t_en: Truly sorry, good guest. That was the last of our available inventory. I will inform you when the new stock arrives.

t_ch: 这是一种浴池添加物，据说能让使用者经历类似于黄金裔「领受神谕」时的体验。另外，好像…只剩最后一件了，是个难得的机会呢，客人。
t_en: This is a type of bath additive that is said to grant the user an experience equal to the time when the prophecy was bestowed upon the Chrysos Heirs. Also, it appears that... this is the last in stock. A most rare opportunity, my good guest.

t_ch: 我买了！
t_en: I'll take it!

t_ch: 算了吧。
t_en: Forget it.

t_ch: 那就让它接着吃灰吧。还有别的需要吗，客人？
t_en: Let it keep gathering dust then. Do you need anything else, good guest?

t_ch: 没什么想买的。
t_en: I have nothing to buy.

t_ch: 好的，愿您在云石天宫里洗去疲惫，客人。
t_en: Understood. May your fatigue be washed away in Marmoreal Palace, good guest.

t_ch: 事不宜迟，你回到私人浴池，想要立刻试试……
t_en: With no time to waste, you head back to the private bath, eager to try it out...

t_ch: 水质柔软，泡沫适量，浴盐带来的淡淡花香又为室内增添了些许温馨氛围。啊，还有什么比旅途劳顿之后泡个澡更为放松的事呢？
t_en: You bath in the soft water in the embrace of nice and rich bubbles, the faint floral fragrance of the bath salts adds a certain cozy touch to the room. Ah, is there anything more relaxing than a bubble bath after a long, labored journey?

t_ch: 虽然不知道「领受神谕时的体验」是不是和公司商品一样的常见噱头，但浴盐本身的品质就已经让它物有所值了——只要没有面前这坨会说话的雾。
t_en: You don't know if an "experience akin to when the prophecy was bestowed" is another one of those gimmicky slogans similar to the commercial spam, but the quality of the bath salts itself proves their worth — if not for the talking mist in front of you.

t_ch: 啊，孩子，在我的庭院里过得还舒适吗？
t_en: Ah, child, are you doing well in my courtyard?

t_ch: 你看起来有些疑惑，在你面前的是勒法刻，全翁罗法斯最最伟大的泰坦。要想拿留影石机就趁现在了。
t_en: You look confused — before you stands Lephake, the greatest Titan in all of Omlophas. If you want to get the photostone, there is no better time than now.

t_ch: 神谕降临了？
t_en: Is this the moment of prophecy bestowal?

t_ch: 神谕？什么神谕？我是来拉票的。
t_en: Prophecy? What prophecy? I'm just here to drum up support.

t_ch: 勒法刻？不是刻法勒？
t_en: Lephake? Not Kephale?

t_ch: 谁？哦，它啊，它是我兄弟，我们俩长得差不多，但它没我这么高大威武！
t_en: Who? Oh, that's my brother. We look similar, but Kephale's not as majestic and dignified as I am!

t_ch: 你瞧，又到了泰坦人气投票的月份了。这届的竞争相当激烈，老塞的新专辑确实让很多信徒都失了智，但小墨在时装展上的惊艳亮相也为她带来了不少支持者。
t_en: You see, it's the time of the year again for the Titan popularity vote. The competition this time round is quite intense — Ol' Thannie's new album has got all the disciples in a zealous frenzy, and Mnestia's fabulous outfit at the fashion exhibition has garnered plenty of supporters.

t_ch: 不用我说，你应该明白我来找你做什么了吧？要说神谕也确实算。汝将竖耳聆听，虔诚的「白银裔」啊——
t_en: Surely you understand what I'm doing here, no? I guess you may call this a moment of prophecy bestowal anyway. Pray perk your ears and listen, devout "Argyros Heir"...

t_ch: 「投票给蓝色的蘑菇，下次购物满十万将立减十利衡币。」
t_en: "Cast your vote for the blue mushroom, and receive a discount of ten Balance Coins for your next purchase of a hundred thousand."

t_ch: 切记，切记，切记……
t_en: Remember, remember, remember...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你还好吗？你已经在浴池里待了超过两个时刻了。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, are you all right? You've been in the bath for more than two hours.

t_ch: 摇了摇仍旧有些发昏的头，你重新回到了公共浴场……
t_en: Shaking your still-dizzy head, you notice you have returned to the public bath...

t_ch: 您这个表情…难道是见到扎格列斯本尊了？之前每一位买下来的客人都和您差不多反应…真是太令人好奇了。
t_en: That face of yours... Did you meet Zagreus in person? Every customer who bought this reacted similarly to you... How curious.

t_ch: 总之，谢谢惠顾，欢迎下次再来。
t_en: In any case, thank you for your patronage. Hope to see you again soon.

t_ch: 热情洋溢的异乡人啊，你如此步履匆匆
t_en: O passionate outlander, your pace is in such hurry

t_ch: 奥赫玛敞亮的天光里，随处可见你身影
t_en: Your form is seen everywhere within the divine light of Okhema

t_ch: 你穿行街巷、流连市井；却又与半神比肩、与英雄同行
t_en: You traverse the streets and bustle the crowds, yet stand shoulder to shoulder with demigods and heroes

t_ch: 聆听「门径」之颂歌、见证「纷争」之新生、围猎恶徒于悬锋…
t_en: Listen to the hymns of Passage, witness the rebirth of Strife, and hunt down the wicked in Kremnos...

t_ch: 凡人终生难遇其一，如是精彩纷呈；而你——
t_en: A mortal may encounter such brilliance only once in a lifetime. Yet you...

t_ch: 你尚有前途漫漫，方才启程
t_en: Your journey has just begun, your path lies yonder ahead of you.

t_ch: 《异乡人之歌》
t_en: The Outlander's Ballads

t_ch: 公民大会上人声鼎沸，半神与元老针锋相对
t_en: Voices fill the citizens' assembly as demigods and Elders stand in opposition.

t_ch: 有的希求黄金的旧影，有的寻觅再创的门扉
t_en: Some yearn for the golden past of Era Chrysea, while others seek the new dawn of Era Nova.

t_ch: 可瞧啊——
t_en: But you see—

t_ch: 渎神者反成了神之眷属，送葬者终将令死亡回归
t_en: A blasphemer becomes Titankin, while an undertaker brings death home.

t_ch: 逐火苦旅尚未停歇，我等岂可止步后退
t_en: Our Flame-Chase Journey goes on. We must not falter.

t_ch: 《再创者之歌》
t_en: Epic of the Era Nova

t_ch: 隔着百步之距时，金线就已预知贵客的到来。{NICKNAME}阁下，想在此休息会儿吗？
t_en: The golden thread had already foreseen your arrival when you were a hundred steps away, dear customer. Esteemed {NICKNAME}, have you come to rest?

t_ch: 想同你聊些事情。
t_en: I wish to discuss some things with you.

t_ch: 随意问吧。敞开心扉的闲谈，有时比起沐浴更能令人身心松弛。
t_en: Ask as you please. There are times when speaking from the heart can be even more relaxing than a good, warm bath.

t_ch: 领导逐火之旅，很劳累吧？
t_en: Leading the Flame-Chase Journey must be exhausting.

t_ch: 处理奥赫玛大小事务、为逐火之旅奔波，或许确实是疲累的生活，但于我而言，不过是重复了无数光阴的日常罢了。
t_en: Dealing with the affairs of Okhema and being constantly on the move for the Flame-Chase Journey could perhaps be considered a tiring life. To me, however, it is no more than a daily routine that I have repeated for ages.

t_ch: 劳累的感触对我来说还真有些遥远了。我记得年幼时，半夜馋嘴偷吃神殿里的燕麦粥，被逮住后罚站到天明。那夜我肌酸骨痛，倒是真切地将「累」的感触刻进心中了。
t_en: The feeling of exhaustion is one that feels distant to me. I remember when I was young that I greedily snuck into the temple to eat the offerings of oatmeal. I was caught in the act and was punished. I stood in the temple until dawn. That night, my muscles burned and my bones ached. That was when the sensation of "exhaustion" was truly burned into my bones.

t_ch: 原来「金织」大人也有黑历史！
t_en: Even the "Goldweaver" has embarrassing childhood stories!

t_ch: 是啊，每个人都有稚趣盎然的时候，但终究会随着{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}的步伐流逝，只存于追忆之中。
t_en: That's right. We were all young. However, those days shall eventually pass with the march of {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#} to exist only as memories.

t_ch: 我都不敢想那燕麦粥该多好吃！
t_en: I can only imagine how delicious those oats must have been!

t_ch: 那确实是暌违已久的美味了。我记得那是吾师与我初见时亲手烹饪的，还放了雅努萨波利斯的特产。
t_en: They did indeed have a wonderful flavor that I have not enjoyed for a long time since. I remember my teachers cooked them the first time that we met. They even added some Janusopolis specialty ingredients.

t_ch: 但如今雅努萨波利斯已殁于黄金战争，那滋味也在漫长岁月里消耗殆尽……
t_en: However, Janusopolis perished in the Chrysos War and that delicious flavor has long since been lost...

t_ch: 自从归还火种，成为人们口中的半神，曾经刻骨铭心的感触都已如烛烟袅袅散去，这颗承载着世界命运的心也愈发冰冷。兴许不久后，连沐浴都不能使我欢欣了。
t_en: Since returning the Coreflame and becoming what people refer to as a demigod, sensations that were once deeply engraved on my heart have faded away like candle smoke. This heart that bears the destiny of this world has also grown cold. Perhaps soon, not even bathing will bring me joy any longer.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅女士的爱好？
t_en: What are your hobbies, Lady Aglaea?

t_ch: 如你所见，沐浴。虽然我少有疲惫的心境，但躯体因劳碌产生的沉重之感却会不断积累。定期前来浴场，正是为了将其纾解。
t_en: As you can see, I enjoy bathing. Although I rarely experience fatigue, the feeling of heaviness in my body from a busy life does accumulate endlessly. I come to the baths periodically to relieve this burden.

t_ch: 裁布纳衣亦是我所钟情之事。经纬翻飞之间织出与世人之美相衬的华服，能为我带来珍贵的满足感。
t_en: Tailoring is also a passion of mine. Weaving warp and weft to create costumes befitting the beauty of this world brings me a precious feeling of satisfaction.

t_ch: 不过…我对衣物的品质要求颇高，称得上完工的成衣屈指可数，大部分都被废弃。
t_en: However... I have extremely high requirements for the quality of garments. My works that meet my standards are few and far between, the rest are all discarded.

t_ch: 而那些瑕疵品总会莫名流入奥赫玛的服装市场，标注着我的名字被高价拍卖，成为某位王公贵族的收藏。我虽不乐得见此，但也无权禁止，就随他们去吧。
t_en: These rejected items always inexplicably end up flowing into Okhema's clothing market. They're tagged with my name and auctioned for high prices to be added to some aristocrat's collection. Though I do not like this, I cannot forbid it. They can do as they please.

t_ch: 你愿意给他人做衣服吗？
t_en: Would you make clothes for others?

t_ch: 有何不可呢？但我希望自己的造物能有与之相称的主人。
t_en: Why not? But I do hope there would be people that befit my works.

t_ch: 「浪漫」的承载者、演绎者、求索者、理解者——皆可得我亲手量体裁衣，这亦是我对墨涅塔神权的延续。
t_en: Those that bear, interpret, seek, and understand "romance"... They are worthy of my hand-made garments. This is my continuation of Mnestia's divine authority.

t_ch: 评价一下我的衣品！
t_en: Please rate my clothing!

t_ch: 以我所见，阁下的仪态十分「美丽」…这并非讽喻或奉承，服装审美有高低之别而无美丑之分，穿衣之人则有美丑之分而无高低之别。
t_en: I can see that you are a person of "fine" bearing, {F#Lady}{M#Lord} {NICKNAME}. This is no satire or flattery. The disparities of aesthetics lie in the choices of clothing, not the wearers, while the differences of beauty are among the wearers, not their choices of clothing.

t_ch: 最为夺目的华服，穿在人面兽心者身上，也遮蔽不住丑陋；相反，一眼望去朴素无华的服饰，若与适合的人共鸣，也能大放异彩。
t_en: Even the most eye-catching finery when draped across the body of a person with an evil heart cannot conceal their ugliness. By contrast, if completely unremarkable clothing is paired with someone who resonates perfectly, it can create a spectacular image.

t_ch: 譬如阁下所着，若仅论其用料与织工，我自然能挑出无数瑕疵。
t_en: When it comes to your clothing, I can naturally point out countless flaws in simply the material and craftsmanship.

t_ch: 但沿着金丝，我能感受到这套衣裳上的「浪漫」——庞鸿而不紊乱，如同交汇而不拥堵的道路。我并无信心做出比它更能承载阁下光辉的服饰。
t_en: But by following the golden thread, I can sense the "romance" in it... Convoluted but not chaotic, like a bustling crossroad that is never stalled with traffic. I am confident that it would be impossible for me to create anything that would be more suited to your glory.

t_ch: 对黄金裔们的评价？
t_en: What do you think of the Chrysos Heirs?

t_ch: 纷争世以来的漫长岁月，有无数世人、无数眷属、无数黄金裔与我、与奥赫玛、与泰坦们产生了命运的交织。不知你要问哪些人？
t_en: Over the many long years since the Era Bellica, there have been countless people, Titankins, and Chrysos Heirs who have formed bonds of destiny with myself, Okhema, and the Titans. Which ones are you referring to?

t_ch: 悬锋人中的悬锋人，万敌！
t_en: The most typical of the Kremnoans, Mydei!

t_ch: 悬锋人中的悬锋人…如此评价迈德漠斯倒也合理，他无疑是最英勇的悬锋人，在战场上，谁也不能将其击倒。
t_en: The most typical of the Kremnoans... A fair evaluation of Mydeimos. He is undoubtedly the most heroic warrior among all Kremnoans. He is unstoppable on the battlefield.

t_ch: 但从某种角度上说，他也是最不像悬锋人的悬锋人。
t_en: Though, in some ways, he is the least Kremnoan of all.

t_ch: 超可爱的缇宝、缇宁、缇安。
t_en: The adorable Tribbie, Trinnon, and Trianne.

t_ch: 呵…看来你和奥赫玛的居民们一样，都很喜欢吾师。她们确实是黄金裔中最受奥赫玛平民欢迎者，平日里热衷于与街坊打交道，帮人们解决生活中的疑难琐事。
t_en: Hah... I see you are just as fond of my teachers as the citizens of Okhema are. They indeed enjoy enormous popularity among the people of Okhema. They spend their days deeply committed to communicating with their neighbors and helping people with both great troubles and trivial affairs.

t_ch: 呵呵…若这些温馨的日常能够稍微冲淡她们所负的沉重命运，我便再欣慰不过了。
t_en: Hah... If only such happiness within their daily life could slightly ease the great weight of destiny that they bear... That would bring me true relief.

t_ch: 差点送我去打复活赛的遐蝶。
t_en: Castorice, after our rough encounter.

t_ch: 看来阁下依然对那时的试探耿耿于怀。{NICKNAME}阁下尽可指责我的失礼，但请别对遐蝶心生芥蒂。
t_en: I see that you still bear a grudge over her scrutiny. {F#Lady}{M#Lord} {NICKNAME}, you may by all means hold me accountable for my discourtesy. However, please do not foster resentment against Castorice.

t_ch: 她只是听命于我，愿为自己所追逐的事物奉上一切，也愿为此敞开那夺去生命的怀抱——即便那是最令她迷惘之事。
t_en: She merely heeds my orders and is willing to give everything to obtain what she seeks. Even if that means extending her life-stealing embrace... This can create a confusing paradox for her.

t_ch: 与我十分甚至九分像的白厄。
t_en: My doppelgänger, Phainon.

t_ch: …无缺的英雄。时至今日，我也只能如此评价白厄。作为最完美的黄金裔，他注定在逐火之旅中背负沉重的救世使命。
t_en: ...The perfect hero. To this day, that is the only opinion I have ever been able to make of Phainon. As the most perfect Chrysos Heir, he is fated to bear the heavy fate of saving the world in the Flame-Chase Journey.

t_ch: 等他从创世涡心中凯旋，我们也能离我们的夙愿更进一步。
t_en: When he returns triumphant from the Vortex of Genesis, we will be one step closer to our long-held wish.

t_ch: 都是熟人了，不问了。
t_en: I know everyone well, and there's nothing more I need to know.

t_ch: 无论哪位黄金裔，都是此世不可或缺的英杰。我其实也无权代替这个时代评判他们，因为他们都承载着这个世界的未来。
t_en: Every member of the Chrysos Heirs is a hero that this world cannot do without. I have no right to cast appraisal on them on behalf of this era, because they all shoulder the future of this world.

t_ch: 下次再聊吧。
t_en: Let's catch up another time.

t_ch: 与阁下的交流十分愉快，我很期待再度与你畅谈。
t_en: It's been an absolute delight to speak with you, {F#Lady}{M#Lord} {NICKNAME}. I look forward to our next talk.

t_ch: 下次再聊吧。
t_en: Let's catch up another time.

t_ch: 与阁下的交流十分愉快，很期待再度与你畅谈。
t_en: It's been an absolute delight to speak with you, {F#Lady}{M#Lord} {NICKNAME}. I look forward to our next talk.

t_ch: 呀，小灰！你也来花园这边散心吗？这边风景真的很好，*我们*要是觉得累了，就会来这里远眺。
t_en: Ah, Little Gray! You've come to the garden to relax, too? The scenery here is beautiful. We often come here when feeling overwhelmed.

t_ch: 想和缇宝聊聊天！
t_en: I want to chat with you, Tribbie!

t_ch: 当然可以！小灰想聊些什么呢？又想听*我们*给你唱预言的歌谣吗？
t_en: No problem! What would you like to discuss, Little Gray? Do you want to hear our song of prophecy again?

t_ch: 我老分不清你和缇安、缇宁。
t_en: I can never tell you, Trianne, and Trinnon apart.

t_ch: 别灰心！许多新朋友都会犯糊涂，所以*我们*为他们编了个用于辨认的口诀，小灰听好咯——
t_en: Don't worry! It happens all the time. So, we created a slogan to help people tell the difference. Listen up, Little Gray...

t_ch: 安静的缇宁话最少，活泼的缇安喜欢闹，乖巧的缇宝到处飘！
t_en: Quiet Trinnon has little to say, lively Trianne loves to play, and well-behaved Tribbie roams around all day!

t_ch: 怎么样，这下就不会再认错了吧？
t_en: What do you think? You won't get us mixed up again from now on, right?

t_ch: 举手！想更了解缇安一些！
t_en: I'd like to get to know a bit more about Trianne!

t_ch: 缇安是*我们*三人中最活泼最勇敢的一位啦，虽然和*我们*一样看上去都是小孩子，但她很愿意做一个战士，飞在最前面保护大家！
t_en: Trianne is the liveliest and bravest among us. Although she looks like a child like the rest of us, she always dashes headlong into danger and protect everyone as a brave warrior!

t_ch: 正因如此，缇安才主动接过「门匠」的职责，用「百界门」帮助人们从危险的地方脱身。
t_en: That is why Trianne actively took on the role of "gatekeeper" and uses the "Century Gate" to help people escape from danger.

t_ch: 只是…希望她不要太勉强自己呀。
t_en: I just... I hope that she doesn't overexert herself.

t_ch: 提问！想更了解缇宁一些！
t_en: I want to know more about Trinnon!

t_ch: 缇宁在*我们*之中最为冷静，想问题的时候能考虑得面面俱到，大家都说她身上有一股学者的气质。
t_en: Trinnon is the most cool-headed among us. When she thinks about a problem, she considers every possible angle. Everyone says that she has the makings of a scholar.

t_ch: 要是再在一个和平繁荣的年代，缇宁说不定真的能做一个满肚子学问的好学者、好老师呢！
t_en: If we lived in a time of peace and prosperity, maybe Trinnon could have been a knowledgeable scholar or teacher!

t_ch: 申请！想更了解缇宝一些！
t_en: I would like to know more about you, Tribbie!

t_ch: 哎呀…自己评价自己，总觉得有些怪怪的。大家都说缇宝最为乖巧，可在*我们*看来，缇安和缇宁也都很乖呀。
t_en: Um... You want to hear Tribbie's comment on Tribbie, huh? Kind of an odd request but sure. Everyone says that Tribbie is the most well-behaved of us. But, we think that Trianne and Trinnon are well-behaved, too.

t_ch: 有时候人们明明说不出理由，却能在心里精准地勾勒出对一个人的印象。比如在*我们*眼中，小灰就特别可靠，是和阿雅和小白他们不一样的那种可靠！
t_en: Sometimes people have a clearly defined idea of a person but cannot give an exact reason for it. In our eyes, you are an extremely reliable person, Little Gray! Though you are not Agy or Snowy, you are just as reliable as they are.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，不知道在小灰眼里，缇宝是什么样的呢？
t_en: Hehe, Tribbie wonders what she's like in your eyes.

t_ch: 小灰要是实在分不清，其实还有一个笨办法……
t_en: If you still cannot tell the difference, there is actually a simpler method...

t_ch: 那就是看*我们*的发型！具体怎么辨别，*我们*就不多说啦，小灰眼睛那么尖，一定能发现的！
t_en: Look at our hairstyles! You're observant so we don't need to explain in too much detail. We know you'll see the difference in no time!

t_ch: 缇宝老师，我想学「百界门」！
t_en: Master Tribbie, I want to learn how to use the "Century Gate"!

t_ch: 小灰，好有志气！可普通人怎么想都不可能学会「百界门」的啦。
t_en: Ambitious, Gray Hair! It is impossible for ordinary folks to understand how to use the "Century Gate."

t_ch: …不过小灰只是看几眼就能学会欧洛尼斯奇迹，听上去也是一件不可能的事情…难道，小灰真的是天才？
t_en: ...That being said, you took one look at Oronyx's Miracle and learned how to use it. That should've been equally impossible... Could Tribbie be wrong and you are a genius all along?

t_ch: 好，决定了，*我们*来教你使用百界门，来创造奇迹吧！
t_en: Alright, it's decided. We will teach you how to use the Century Gate! Let's create some miracles!
